Laat 10 minuten rusten voor het serveren.

name: High-protein chocolate mousse
instructions: Peel the hard-boiled eggs
Add eggs, plant-based milk, cacao powder, maple syrup and vanilla to a blender
Blend until completely smooth and creamy
Transfer to a serving glass or bowl
Refrigerate for 30 minutes for best texture, or serve immediately
recipe_benefits: High protein from eggs supports muscle recovery
Antioxidants from raw cacao
Satisfies chocolate cravings with minimal sugar
recipe_tips: Use cold hard-boiled eggs for best texture
Add more maple syrup if you prefer sweeter
Top with cacao nibs or berries
dutch_instructions: Pel de hardgekookte eieren.
Doe eieren, plantaardige melk, cacaopoeder, ahornsiroop en vanille in een blender.
Blend tot volledig glad en romig.
Schep over in een serveerglas of kom.
Zet 30 minuten in de koelkast voor de beste textuur, of serveer direct.

name: Peanut butter chocolate bars
instructions: Mix oat flour, 11g peanut butter, 6g maple syrup, 2g coconut oil and a pinch of salt until it forms a soft dough
Press evenly into a small parchment-lined container
Chill for 15-20 minutes
Gently melt remaining peanut butter, coconut oil and maple syrup together until smooth
Pour over the base and spread evenly
Chill again for 10-15 minutes
Melt the dark chocolate with remaining coconut oil
Stir in chopped roasted peanuts
Spread over the peanut butter layer evenly
Refrigerate 1 hour or until firm
Slice into bars and store chilled
recipe_benefits: Healthy fats from peanut butter and coconut oil
Plant protein from peanuts
Antioxidants from dark chocolate
recipe_tips: Make a full batch (8x the recipe) in a 20x20cm pan for meal prep
Store in fridge for up to 1 week
Can use almond flour instead of oat flour
dutch_instructions: Mix oat flour, 11g peanut butter, 6g maple syrup, 2g coconut oil and a pinch of salt until it forms a soft dough
Press evenly into a small parchment-lined container
Chill for 15-20 minutes
Gently melt remaining peanut butter, coconut oil and maple syrup together until smooth
Pour over the base and spread evenly
Chill again for 10-15 minutes
Melt the dark chocolate with remaining coconut oil
Stir in chopped roasted peanuts
Spread over the peanut butter layer evenly
Refrigerate 1 hour or until firm
Slice into bars and store chilled

name: Matcha pistachio bliss balls
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine almond flour, pistachio butter, maple syrup, matcha powder and vanilla extract
Stir until the mixture comes together into a soft, dough-like consistency
If too sticky, add more almond flour; if too dry, add more pistachio butter or maple syrup
Roll the mixture into small bite-sized balls using your hands
Lightly dust each ball with extra matcha powder for garnish
Refrigerate for 30 minutes to firm up
recipe_benefits: Healthy fats from almonds and pistachios
Antioxidants from matcha green tea
Natural energy boost without caffeine crash
recipe_tips: Make a full batch (5x the recipe) for meal prep snacks
Store in fridge for up to 1 week
Can use pistachio matcha crema if available for extra flavor
dutch_instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine almond flour, pistachio butter, maple syrup, matcha powder and vanilla extract
Stir until the mixture comes together into a soft, dough-like consistency
If too sticky, add more almond flour; if too dry, add more pistachio butter or maple syrup
Roll the mixture into small bite-sized balls using your hands
Lightly dust each ball with extra matcha powder for garnish
Refrigerate for 30 minutes to firm up

name: Smashed potato tacos
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a baking tray
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil, add the potatoes and boil for 20 minutes or until soft
Drain and set aside for 5 minutes
Place the potatoes on the tray ensuring none are touching, and use the base of a glass to press down on each potato, keeping them in one piece
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper
Bake for 45-50 minutes or until golden and crispy, do not turn over
Mash the avocado and dice the cherry tomatoes
Top each smashed potato with avocado, black beans, cherry tomatoes and crumbled vegan feta
Garnish with fresh coriander and pickled onion
recipe_benefits: High fiber from black beans and potatoes
Heart-healthy fats from avocado
Plant protein from beans
recipe_tips: Use small waxy potatoes for best results
Can substitute regular feta if not vegan
Make extra pickled onions to store in the fridge
dutch_instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a baking tray
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil, add the potatoes and boil for 20 minutes or until soft
Drain and set aside for 5 minutes
Place the potatoes on the tray ensuring none are touching, and use the base of a glass to press down on each potato, keeping them in one piece
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper
Bake for 45-50 minutes or until golden and crispy, do not turn over
Mash the avocado and dice the cherry tomatoes
Top each smashed potato with avocado, black beans, cherry tomatoes and crumbled vegan feta
Garnish with fresh coriander and pickled onion

name: Mung bean wraps
instructions: Wash mung beans with water and drain
Soak the mung beans with filtered water in an airtight container and place in the fridge overnight or at least 8 hours
Drain the mung beans and pour into a high-speed blender with the water
Blend on high speed until the batter is a smooth consistency
Heat a medium-sized pan over low-medium heat, spray a bit of oil if not non-stick
Ladle a scoop of batter into the pan and form a circle with the ladle
Cook for 3 minutes before flipping the wrap over
Cook the other side for 1-2 minutes
Remove the wrap and set aside
Use immediately or store in an airtight container
recipe_benefits: High protein plant-based wrap alternative
Excellent source of plant fiber
Low glycemic index for stable blood sugar
recipe_tips: Make a full batch (8 wraps) for weekly meal prep
Store in airtight container for up to 5 days
Fill with vegetables, hummus, or protein of choice
Soak beans overnight for best results
dutch_instructions: Wash mung beans with water and drain
Soak the mung beans with filtered water in an airtight container and place in the fridge overnight or at least 8 hours
Drain the mung beans and pour into a high-speed blender with the water
Blend on high speed until the batter is a smooth consistency
Heat a medium-sized pan over low-medium heat, spray a bit of oil if not non-stick
Ladle a scoop of batter into the pan and form a circle with the ladle
Cook for 3 minutes before flipping the wrap over
Cook the other side for 1-2 minutes
Remove the wrap and set aside
Use immediately or store in an airtight container

name: Soul cacao
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a small pan
Gently warm the cacao, keeping it below 80°C
Whisk until creamy
Pour into your favorite cup and enjoy
recipe_benefits: Rich in antioxidants from raw cacao
Mood-boosting theobromine
Anti-inflammatory spices cardamom and cinnamon
recipe_tips: Use high-quality ceremonial cacao for best flavor
Keep temperature below 80°C to preserve nutrients
Can substitute agave for maple syrup
dutch_instructions: Combine all ingredients in a small pan
Gently warm the cacao, keeping it below 80°C
Whisk until creamy
Pour into your favorite cup and enjoy

name: Wieringer bean stew with grilled tofu
instructions: If using dried beans, soak 8-12 hours, drain, rinse, then cook in fresh water for 45-60 minutes until tender
Heat olive oil in a pan and sauté onion and garlic until translucent
Add leek and cook until softened
Add chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, chili flakes and thyme, simmer 1-2 minutes
Add beans and vegetable stock to just cover
Simmer on low heat for 15-20 minutes so flavors combine
Season with salt and pepper
Heat a grill pan with a little oil over medium-high heat
Season tofu with salt and pepper
Fry tofu 3-4 minutes per side until browned and slightly crispy
Spoon bean stew into a deep plate
Place grilled tofu on top
Finish with sumac and a squeeze of lemon juice
recipe_benefits: Complete plant protein from beans and tofu
High fiber supports digestive health
Sumac adds antioxidants and tangy flavor
recipe_tips: Wieringer beans are a Dutch heirloom variety - use white beans or cannellini as substitute
Press tofu well for crispier results
Can use passata instead of chopped tomatoes
dutch_name: Wieringer bean stew with grilled tofu
dutch_instructions: If using dried beans, soak 8-12 hours, drain, rinse, then cook in fresh water for 45-60 minutes until tender
Heat olive oil in a pan and sauté onion and garlic until translucent
Add leek and cook until softened
Add chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, chili flakes and thyme, simmer 1-2 minutes
Add beans and vegetable stock to just cover
Simmer on low heat for 15-20 minutes so flavors combine
Season with salt and pepper
Heat a grill pan with a little oil over medium-high heat
Season tofu with salt and pepper
Fry tofu 3-4 minutes per side until browned and slightly crispy
Spoon bean stew into a deep plate
Place grilled tofu on top
Finish with sumac and a squeeze of lemon juice

name: Golden tofu and aubergine curry
instructions: To make the curry paste, add 1 tablespoon of coconut milk, the ginger, coriander stalks, shallots, coriander seeds, turmeric, chilli flakes, black pepper and 1 tablespoon of water to a blender. Blitz until smooth.
Warm the oil in a large frying pan over medium-low heat. Add the curry paste and cook for 10 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant.
Increase the heat slightly, add the aubergine and cook for 5 minutes until just starting to soften, adding a little more oil if needed.
Stir in the remaining coconut milk, tofu, maple syrup, tamari, 200ml water and salt. Simmer for 20 minutes until the aubergine is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Add the sugar snap peas for the final 3-4 minutes of cooking.
Stir in the lime juice, taste and adjust seasoning. Scatter fresh coriander leaves over the curry before serving. Delicious with rice noodles or steamed basmati rice.
dutch_instructions: Maak de currypasta door 1 eetlepel kokosmelk, de gember, korianderstengels, sjalotten, korianderzaad, kurkuma, chilivlokken, zwarte peper en 1 eetlepel water in een blender te doen. Blend tot glad.
Verwarm de olie in een grote koekenpan op middelhoog vuur. Voeg de currypasta toe en bak 10 minuten, regelmatig roerend, tot geurig.
Verhoog het vuur iets, voeg de aubergine toe en bak 5 minuten tot deze net zacht begint te worden, voeg indien nodig wat extra olie toe.
Roer de rest van de kokosmelk, tofu, ahornsiroop, tamari, 200ml water en zout erdoor. Laat 20 minuten sudderen tot de aubergine zacht is en de saus is ingedikt.
Voeg de sugarsnaps toe voor de laatste 3-4 minuten van het koken.
Roer het limoensap erdoor, proef en breng op smaak. Strooi verse korianderblaadjes over de curry voor het serveren. Heerlijk met rijstnoedels of gestoomde basmatirijst.

name: Pumpkin-coconut pasta
instructions: Rehydrate the dried soy mince in 100ml hot vegetable stock for 10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Peel the pumpkin (optional) and cut into small cubes. Finely chop the onion and garlic.
Boil the pumpkin cubes for 10 minutes until soft. Drain, return to the pan, and blend into a smooth purée with a stick blender.
Heat a frying pan with oil and cook the onion for 8 minutes. Add garlic, curry leaves, and rehydrated soy mince. Cook 3 minutes more. Set half of the curry leaves aside on kitchen paper for garnish.
Add coconut milk, pumpkin purée, stock cube, spices, olives, and lemon juice to the pan. Stir and simmer on low heat.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions. Drain and mix with the sauce.
Serve topped with the reserved curry leaves and toasted pine nuts.
dutch_instructions: Laat het gedroogde sojagehakt 10 minuten weken in 100ml hete groentebouillon. Laat uitlekken en zet apart.
Schil de pompoen (optioneel) en snijd in kleine blokjes. Snipper de ui en knoflook fijn.
Kook de pompoenblokjes 10 minuten tot ze zacht zijn. Giet af, doe terug in de pan en pureer glad met een staafmixer.

Verhit een koekenpan met olie en bak de ui 8 minuten. Voeg knoflook, kerryblaadjes en het geweekte sojagehakt toe. Bak nog 3 minuten. Leg de helft van de kerryblaadjes apart op keukenpapier voor garnering.
Voeg kokosmelk, pompoenpuree, bouillonblokje, kruiden, olijven en citroensap toe aan de pan. Roer en laat op laag vuur sudderen.
Kook de pasta volgens de verpakking. Giet af en meng met de saus.
Serveer met de achtergehouden kerryblaadjes en geroosterde pijnboompitten.

name: Caramelised pear porridge with walnuts
instructions: Cut the pear, remove the core, and slice into thin wedges.
Add the coconut oil and maple syrup to a pan on low heat. Add the pear slices and cook for about 5 minutes. Add the walnuts and cook for a few more minutes until caramelised, stirring occasionally.
Transfer the pear and walnuts to a plate to cool.
Add the oats and plant-based milk to a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Simmer on low heat for about 5 minutes, stirring continuously, until it becomes porridge.
Serve the porridge topped with the caramelised pear, walnuts, and a pinch of cinnamon.
dutch_instructions: Snijd de peer, verwijder het klokhuis en snijd in dunne partjes.

Voeg de kokosolie en ahornsiroop toe aan een pan op laag vuur. Voeg de perenpartjes toe en bak ongeveer 5 minuten. Voeg de walnoten toe en bak nog een paar minuten tot gekaramelliseerd, af en toe roeren.
Leg de peer en walnoten op een bord om af te koelen.
Voeg de havermout en plantaardige melk toe aan een kleine steelpan en breng aan de kook. Laat op laag vuur ongeveer 5 minuten sudderen, continu roerend, tot het pap wordt.
Serveer de havermout met de gekaramelliseerde peer, walnoten en een snufje kaneel.

name: Chocolate cardamom cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 160°C (fan/convection).
Put the ground flaxseed in a glass with 45ml water. Stir and let sit 5-10 minutes until thickened.
Crush the cardamom pods in a mortar. Remove the green husks and grind the seeds finely.
In a bowl, mix the almond butter, coconut blossom sugar, and the flax mixture with a spoon.
Add the cocoa powder, oat flour, baking powder, baking soda, 45ml water, ground cardamom, and salt. Knead with your hands until you have a smooth ball of dough.
Divide the dough into 8 pieces and roll into balls.
Place on a baking tray lined with parchment paper and bake 15 minutes. Let cool so they crisp up. Sprinkle with a pinch of coarse sea salt before serving.
dutch_instructions: Verwarm de oven voor op 160°C (hetelucht).
Doe het gemalen lijnzaad in een glas met 45ml water. Roer en laat 5-10 minuten staan tot het is ingedikt.
Plet de kardemompeulen in een vijzel. Verwijder de groene schillen en maal de zaadjes fijn.
Meng in een kom de amandelpasta, kokosbloesemsuiker en het lijnzaadmengsel met een lepel.
Voeg het cacaopoeder, havermeel, bakpoeder, baksoda, 45ml water, gemalen kardemom en zout toe. Kneed met je handen tot je een gladde deegbal hebt.
Verdeel het deeg in 8 stukken en rol tot balletjes.
Leg op een bakplaat met bakpapier en bak 15 minuten. Laat afkoelen zodat ze knapperig worden. Bestrooi voor het serveren met een snufje grof zeezout.

name: Ayurvedic amaranth porridge with roasted figs
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
Bring the plant-based milk and water to a boil, then add the raisins and speculaas spice mix.
Add the amaranth and simmer for about 25 minutes until you have a soft, creamy porridge. Stir well and add extra milk and/or water if needed.
Score the figs in a cross pattern and place them in an oven dish greased with ghee.
Add a little ghee on top of the figs and place the star anise and cinnamon sticks in and around the figs.
Bake the figs for about 15 minutes.
Toast the nuts for 2-3 minutes in a pan with ghee.
Wash an orange and grate a little of the peel (zest).
Fill a bowl with the creamy amaranth, place the figs on top, and finish with the toasted nuts, date syrup, and a little orange zest.
dutch_instructions: Verwarm de oven voor op 180°C.
Breng de plantaardige melk en water aan de kook, voeg dan de rozijnen en speculaaskruiden toe.
Voeg de amaranth toe en laat ongeveer 25 minuten sudderen tot je een zachte, romige pap hebt. Roer goed en voeg extra melk en/of water toe indien nodig.
Snijd de vijgen kruislings in en leg ze in een ovenschaal ingevet met ghee.
Doe wat ghee bovenop de vijgen en leg de steranijs en kaneelstokjes in en rond de vijgen.
Bak de vijgen ongeveer 15 minuten.
Rooster de noten 2-3 minuten in een pan met ghee.
Was een sinaasappel en rasp een beetje van de schil.
Vul een kom met de romige amaranth, leg de vijgen erop en werk af met de geroosterde noten, dadelstroop en wat sinaasappelrasp.

name: Autumn dal with pumpkin and wholegrain rice
instructions: Cook the wholegrain rice separately according to package instructions.
Rinse the mung dal until the water runs clear.
Heat the ghee in a pan. Add cumin, coriander seeds, and mustard seeds; cook until they start to pop.
Add ginger, turmeric, and asafoetida; cook briefly until fragrant.
Add the rinsed mung dal and stir so it is coated with the spices.
Add the vegetable stock and bring to a boil.
Dice the carrot and pumpkin. Add to the pan. Simmer gently for 25-30 minutes, until the dal is cooked and the vegetables are soft.
Season with salt and add lemon juice.
Stir in spinach at the end to wilt. Top with fresh coriander and serve with the wholegrain rice.
dutch_name: Herfstige dal met pompoen en volkoren rijst
dutch_instructions: Kook de volkoren rijst apart volgens de verpakking.
Spoel de mungdal tot het water helder is.
Verhit de ghee in een pan. Voeg komijn, korianderzaad en mosterdzaad toe; bak tot ze beginnen te poppen.
Voeg gember, kurkuma en asafoetida toe; bak kort tot geurig.
Voeg de gespoelde mungdal toe en roer zodat deze bedekt is met de kruiden.
Voeg de groentebouillon toe en breng aan de kook.
Snijd de wortel en pompoen in blokjes. Voeg toe aan de pan. Laat 25-30 minuten zachtjes sudderen tot de dal gaar is en de groenten zacht zijn.
Breng op smaak met zout en voeg citroensap toe.
Roer op het einde de spinazie erdoor om te laten slinken. Garneer met verse koriander en serveer met de volkoren rijst.

name: Black coconut-vanilla rice pudding
instructions: Rinse the black rice thoroughly in a sieve.
Cook the black rice in plenty of boiling water for about 40 minutes until done. Drain, then let it sit for 10 minutes with the lid on the pan.
Put 3/4 of the cooked black rice in a blender. Add the coconut milk, vanilla extract, and coconut blossom sugar. Blend until smooth.
Stir the remaining cooked black rice into the blended mixture and finish with coconut flakes.
dutch_instructions: Rinse the black rice thoroughly in a sieve.
Cook the black rice in plenty of boiling water for about 40 minutes until done. Drain, then let it sit for 10 minutes with the lid on the pan.
Put 3/4 of the cooked black rice in a blender. Add the coconut milk, vanilla extract, and coconut blossom sugar. Blend until smooth.
Stir the remaining cooked black rice into the blended mixture and finish with coconut flakes.

name: Turmeric pancakes
instructions: Blend rice milk, apple cider vinegar, buckwheat flour, rice flour, baking powder, salt, turmeric, and cinnamon until smooth
Beat the egg in a separate bowl and stir into the batter
Heat a cast-iron pan with safflower oil over medium heat
Pour about 1 ladle of batter per pancake and fry until bubbles form on top
Flip and cook until golden brown on both sides
Serve with honey and a pinch of cinnamon
recipe_tips: Use a cast-iron pan for best results and even cooking
Don't flip until bubbles appear and edges look set
Add optional dried dandelion as topping for extra spring energy
These pancakes are naturally gluten-free thanks to buckwheat and rice flour
recipe_notes: Traditional Ayurvedic spring recipe. Turmeric provides anti-inflammatory benefits while buckwheat offers complete protein and magnesium. The warm spices support digestion and the light flours are perfect for spring's kapha-reducing needs.
dutch_instructions: Blend rice milk, apple cider vinegar, buckwheat flour, rice flour, baking powder, salt, turmeric, and cinnamon until smooth
Beat the egg in a separate bowl and stir into the batter
Heat a cast-iron pan with safflower oil over medium heat
Pour about 1 ladle of batter per pancake and fry until bubbles form on top
Flip and cook until golden brown on both sides
Serve with honey and a pinch of cinnamon

name: Sweet Potato and Bean Traybake with Roasted Garlic Yoghurt
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C
Add the sweet potato, mushrooms, red onion, paprika, oregano, 5ml olive oil, 1g salt and black pepper to a baking tray and toss together
Wrap 2 garlic cloves (unpeeled) in foil with 2ml olive oil and tuck into corner of baking tray
Place the tray in the oven and cook for 30 minutes, stirring halfway, until golden
Remove the tray from the oven, then stir in the black beans, butter beans, cherry tomatoes and ginger
Return to the oven for 15-20 minutes, until the tomato skins begin to burst
For the garlic yoghurt: carefully squeeze the flesh of the roasted garlic into a small bowl
Stir the garlic into the coconut yoghurt and add 0.5g salt
Top the vegetables and beans with lime juice, chilli flakes, fresh coriander and 1g sea salt
Serve with the roasted garlic yoghurt on the side

name: Pumpkin-Spice Chia Pudding with Pear
instructions: Whisk plant-based quark with plant-based yogurt in a bowl
Stir in chia seeds and let rest for 10 minutes
Stir again to prevent clumping
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight
Stir in pumpkin purée and pumpkin-spice mix until well combined
Fold in diced pear
Top with pumpkin seeds, maple syrup drizzle, and sea salt flakes before serving

name: Coconut curry with vegan dumplings
instructions: Cook rice according to package instructions and set aside
Heat a pan or wok over medium heat and add red curry paste
Mince garlic and grate ginger, add to pan and fry for 1-2 minutes
Add broccoli florets, sliced red pepper, baby corn, and edamame beans
Stir-fry vegetables for 3-4 minutes until slightly tender
Pour in coconut milk and add kaffir lime leaf
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes
Add dumplings and cook for another 3-4 minutes until heated through
Season with salt to taste
Serve over rice, garnish with fresh coriander, sesame seeds, and crispy chili oil
recipe_tips: Can use steam oven method: switch to steam mode after initial 25 min baking, then steam dumplings 5-10 min
Kaffir lime leaf adds authentic Thai flavor but can be omitted
Adjust curry paste amount based on heat preference
Great for meal prep - reheat gently to preserve dumpling texture
recipe_notes: Aromatic Thai-inspired curry that can be baked in conventional or steam oven. Vegan dumplings make it hearty and satisfying.

name: Coconut curry with gluten-free dumplings
instructions: Cook rice according to package instructions and set aside
Heat a pan or wok over medium heat and add red curry paste
Mince garlic and grate ginger, add to pan and fry for 1-2 minutes
Add broccoli florets, sliced red pepper, baby corn, and edamame beans
Stir-fry vegetables for 3-4 minutes until slightly tender
Pour in coconut milk and add kaffir lime leaf
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes
Add dumplings and cook for another 3-4 minutes until heated through
Season with salt to taste
Serve over rice, garnish with fresh coriander, sesame seeds, and crispy chili oil

name: Skyr cheesecake with passionfruit and walnuts
instructions: Toast the walnuts for a few minutes in a dry skillet until golden and fragrant. Let them cool

Blend the dates, walnuts, and oats in a food processor until fine. Press the mixture evenly into a small dish or ramekin lined with baking paper. Smooth the surface with the back of a wet spoon. Chill in the fridge
Place the skyr in a bowl and mix until smooth
In a small pan, stir the gelatin into 15ml cold water. Add 1 tablespoon of the skyr mixture. Bring slowly to a boil while stirring, then simmer gently for 2 minutes
Remove from heat, mix in one quarter of the remaining skyr, then combine with the rest. Pour over the crust and refrigerate to set

In a clean pan, heat 30g of the passionfruit until thawed. Stir in a pinch of gelatin (about 0.4g), bring to a boil, and simmer for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and mix in the remaining 10g of passionfruit
When cooled, pour the passionfruit topping evenly over the cake. Chill for at least 2 hours to set
Serve with fresh red fruit

benefits: Natural sweetness from dates and passionfruit

recipe_benefits: No-bake dessert with impressive presentation
Skyr provides 18g protein and probiotics
Walnuts add healthy fats and omega-3s
Natural sweetness from dates and tropical passionfruit
recipe_tips: Make sure gelatin dissolves completely to avoid lumps
Chill for at least 2 hours for best texture
Use a food processor for smooth walnut-date crust
Passionfruit can be replaced with mango or other tropical fruit
recipe_notes: Elegant no-bake protein cheesecake made with Icelandic skyr. The walnut-date crust provides healthy fats and natural sweetness, while the passionfruit topping adds tropical flavor.

name: Lactose-free skyr cheesecake with passionfruit and walnuts
instructions: Toast the walnuts for a few minutes in a dry skillet until golden and fragrant. Let them cool

Blend the dates, walnuts, and oats in a food processor until fine. Press the mixture evenly into a small dish or ramekin lined with baking paper. Smooth the surface with the back of a wet spoon. Chill in the fridge
Place the lactose-free skyr in a bowl and mix until smooth
In a small pan, stir the gelatin into 15ml cold water. Add 1 tablespoon of the skyr mixture. Bring slowly to a boil while stirring, then simmer gently for 2 minutes
Remove from heat, mix in one quarter of the remaining skyr, then combine with the rest. Pour over the crust and refrigerate to set

In a clean pan, heat 30g of the passionfruit until thawed. Stir in a pinch of gelatin (about 0.4g), bring to a boil, and simmer for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and mix in the remaining 10g of passionfruit
When cooled, pour the passionfruit topping evenly over the cake. Chill for at least 2 hours to set
Serve with fresh red fruit

benefits: High in protein from lactose-free skyr
Natural sweetness from dates and passionfruit

recipe_benefits: Walnuts add healthy fats and omega-3s
recipe_tips: Lactose-free skyr behaves exactly like regular skyr in this recipe
Make sure gelatin dissolves completely to avoid lumps
Chill for at least 2 hours for best texture
Passionfruit can be replaced with mango or other tropical fruit
recipe_notes: Lactose-free version of elegant no-bake protein cheesecake. Made with lactose-free Icelandic skyr for those with lactose intolerance. Same great taste and protein content as the original.

name: Blood orange compote porridge with cacao nibs
instructions: Segment the oranges and set them aside. Squeeze the juice from the membrane into a small saucepan.
Add 125ml water, the maple syrup, clove and cinnamon to the pan. Bring to the boil, then simmer until reduced by half. Set aside to cool, then stir in the orange segments.
Add the oats, seeds, plant-based milk and salt to a saucepan over medium heat. Simmer for 10-15 minutes, stirring often, until softened.
Serve the porridge topped with the blood orange compote, coconut yoghurt, almonds and cacao nibs.
recipe_benefits: Rich in antioxidants from cacao nibs and blood oranges
Seeds provide omega-3 and minerals
recipe_tips: Blood oranges are in season January-April
Regular oranges can be used as substitute

name: Banana oat pancakes with ground almonds
instructions: Place the oats in a food processor and pulse for about 2 minutes to form a dusty flour.
Add the oat flour to a bowl with the ground almonds, cinnamon, salt and chia seeds. Mix well to remove any lumps.
Add the plant-based milk, mashed banana and maple syrup. Mix well until a pancake batter forms. Leave for 10-15 minutes to thicken.

Place a frying pan over medium heat and add the coconut oil. Once warm, add a heaped tablespoon of the pancake mixture to the middle of the pan. Shape into circles and leave untouched for 2-3 minutes until the bottom has cooked.
Flip the pancakes over and cook on the other side for another 1-2 minutes. Serve topped with maple syrup and sliced banana.
recipe_benefits: Chia seeds provide omega-3 and fiber
Ground almonds add protein and healthy fats
recipe_tips: Let the batter rest 10-15 minutes for best texture

name: Kimchi pancakes with sesame dipping sauce
instructions: Place the coconut milk, gram flour, toasted sesame oil, chilli powder, baking powder, 9g sesame seeds and salt into a large bowl and mix well until smooth. Stir through the chopped kimchi.
Place a pan over medium heat and add a drizzle of sesame oil. Once warm, spoon equal amounts of the batter into the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes until golden on one side.
Flip over and cook for another 3 minutes on the other side. Repeat until all of the mixture has finished.
Mix together the sesame oil, tamari, spring onion, red chilli, 3g sesame seeds, brown rice vinegar and maple syrup. Serve with the pancakes.
recipe_benefits: Kimchi provides probiotics for gut health
Gram flour is high in protein and naturally gluten-free
recipe_tips: Let batter rest a few minutes for best texture

name: Seeded crepes with spelt and gram flour
instructions: Sift both flours, baking powder, seeds and salt into a large bowl and stir together.
Gradually whisk in the plant-based milk, vanilla extract and maple syrup until you have a smooth batter.

Warm a drizzle of olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add a ladleful of batter, swirl the mixture around the pan to create a large thin crepe. Cook for 1.5-3 minutes on each side until golden.
Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil to the pan as needed. Wrap finished crepes in a clean tea towel to keep warm.
Serve immediately with your favourite toppings.
recipe_benefits: Mixed seeds provide omega-3 and minerals
Gram flour adds protein
recipe_tips: Warm the pan thoroughly before cooking

name: Seeded crepes with gram flour (gluten-free)
instructions: Sift the rice flour, gram flour, xanthan gum, baking powder, seeds and salt into a large bowl and stir together.
Gradually whisk in the plant-based milk, vanilla extract and maple syrup until you have a smooth batter.

Warm a drizzle of olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add a ladleful of batter, swirl the mixture around the pan to create a large thin crepe. Cook for 1.5-3 minutes on each side until golden.
Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil to the pan as needed. Wrap finished crepes in a clean tea towel to keep warm.
Serve immediately with your favourite toppings.
recipe_benefits: Mixed seeds provide omega-3 and minerals
Gram flour adds protein
recipe_tips: Xanthan gum helps bind the gluten-free flours for better texture

name: Creamy tomato gnocchi
instructions: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add the gnocchi and cook for 2-3 minutes until they float. Before draining, scoop out about 60ml of the cooking water and set aside.
In a large non-stick skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped red onion and cook for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the cherry tomatoes to the pan and cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they start to burst and release their juices.
Stir in the tomato paste and chili flakes, then season with salt and pepper. Cook for another minute to deepen the flavor.
Pour in the heavy cream and a splash of the reserved gnocchi cooking water. Stir gently and let the sauce simmer for 1-2 minutes until slightly thickened and glossy.
Add the cooked gnocchi straight to the skillet and toss gently to coat in the sauce. If the sauce feels too thick, add a little more pasta water to loosen it up.
Tear the mozzarella into pieces and scatter over the gnocchi. Let it melt slightly into the warm sauce.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh basil leaves and serve immediately.
recipe_tips: Save some gnocchi cooking water - the starchy water helps bind the sauce to the gnocchi
Let the cherry tomatoes burst naturally for the best flavor
Tear the mozzarella by hand for those irresistible cheese pulls
recipe_notes: Store-bought potato gnocchi works perfectly for this quick one-pan recipe. The combination of burst cherry tomatoes, cream, and melted mozzarella creates a restaurant-quality dish in under 20 minutes.

name: Pasta e fagioli with walnut pesto
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a large, deep saucepan set over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the onion, carrot and a pinch of salt. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened but not coloured.
Add the tomatoes, cannellini beans, miso paste and vegetable stock. Simmer for 20 minutes until slightly thickened and the carrots are tender.
Add the pasta and cavolo nero. Cook for 10-12 minutes until the pasta is al dente.
Meanwhile, make the pesto: blend the parsley, walnuts, garlic, olive oil, chilli flakes and salt in a food processor until smooth.
Serve the pasta e fagioli in a bowl, topped with a spoonful of walnut pesto and a drizzle of olive oil.
dutch_instructions: Verwarm de olijfolie in een grote, diepe steelpan op middelhoog vuur. Voeg de knoflook toe en bak 1-2 minuten tot geurig.
Voeg de ui, wortel en een snufje zout toe. Bak 10 minuten, af en toe roerend, tot zacht maar niet gekleurd.
Voeg de tomaten, cannellinibonen, misopasta en groentebouillon toe. Laat 20 minuten sudderen tot licht ingedikt en de wortels zacht zijn.
Voeg de pasta en cavolo nero toe. Kook 10-12 minuten tot de pasta al dente is.
Maak ondertussen de pesto: blend de peterselie, walnoten, knoflook, olijfolie, chilivlokken en zout in een keukenmachine tot glad.
Serveer de pasta e fagioli in een kom, afgewerkt met een lepel walnotenpesto en een scheut olijfolie.

name: Matcha-raspberry chocolate bar
instructions: Line a small tray or plate with baking paper.
If using frozen raspberries, let them sit 5 minutes. Spread raspberries into a tight rectangle about 1-1.5 cm thick. Press gently so berries connect.
Freeze the raspberry layer for 20-30 minutes until firm.
Mix Greek yogurt, matcha powder, honey, vanilla extract, and salt until smooth. Taste and adjust sweetness if needed.
Spread matcha yogurt over the frozen raspberries. Return to freezer for 30-45 minutes until solid.
Melt dark chocolate with coconut oil in short microwave bursts or au bain-marie.
Pour or drizzle chocolate over the frozen matcha layer. Tap tray to level.
Freeze 10-15 minutes until chocolate sets.
Break or slice into bars. Keep frozen for clean pieces. Eat straight from freezer or let sit 2-3 minutes for a softer bite.
dutch_instructions: Bekleed een klein dienblad of bord met bakpapier.
Als je bevroren frambozen gebruikt, laat ze 5 minuten staan. Verdeel de frambozen in een strakke rechthoek van ongeveer 1-1.5 cm dik. Druk zachtjes aan zodat de bessen met elkaar verbinden.
Vries de frambozenlaag 20-30 minuten tot stevig.
Meng Griekse yoghurt, matchapoeder, honing, vanille-extract en zout tot glad. Proef en pas de zoetheid aan indien nodig.
Smeer de matcha-yoghurt over de bevroren frambozen. Zet terug in de vriezer voor 30-45 minuten tot vast.
Smelt pure chocolade met kokosolie in korte magnetronintervallen of au bain-marie.
Giet of druppel de chocolade over de bevroren matchalaag. Tik op het dienblad om te egaliseren.
Vries 10-15 minuten tot de chocolade is uitgehard.
Breek of snijd in repen. Houd bevroren voor schone stukken. Eet direct uit de vriezer of laat 2-3 minuten staan voor een zachtere hap.

name: Coconut chia pudding with protein powder
instructions: Pour the coconut milk into a bowl
Whisk in the protein powder until smooth with no clumps
While whisking, add the chia seeds and mix evenly
Let sit for 15 minutes, stirring a few times to prevent clumping
If the mixture becomes too thick, add a small splash of coconut milk and mix
Top with mixed nuts, berries, coconut flakes, and almond butter as desired
recipe_tips: If your protein powder thickens a lot, increase liquid to 170-200ml to keep a pudding texture
Can be prepared the night before and refrigerated
Stir before serving if refrigerated overnight
recipe_notes: High in omega-3 fatty acids, fiber, and plant-based protein. Excellent for meal prep and menstrual cycle support.
dutch_instructions: Giet de kokosmelk in een kom.
Klop het eiwitpoeder erdoor tot glad zonder klontjes.
Voeg al kloppend de chiazaadjes toe en meng gelijkmatig.
Laat 15 minuten staan, roer een paar keer om klonteren te voorkomen.
Als het mengsel te dik wordt, voeg een scheut kokosmelk toe en meng.
Werk naar wens af met gemengde noten, bessen, kokosvlokken en amandelboter.

name: Coconut chia pudding
instructions: Pour the coconut milk into a bowl
Add the chia seeds while whisking to mix evenly
Let sit for 15 minutes, stirring a few times to prevent clumping
If the mixture becomes too thick, add a small splash of coconut milk and mix
Top with mixed nuts, berries, coconut flakes, and almond butter as desired
recipe_tips: Can be prepared the night before and refrigerated
Stir before serving if refrigerated overnight
Add a natural sweetener like maple syrup if desired
recipe_notes: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids and fiber. Perfect for meal prep and menstrual cycle support.
dutch_instructions: Pour the coconut milk into a bowl
Add the chia seeds while whisking to mix evenly
Let sit for 15 minutes, stirring a few times to prevent clumping
If the mixture becomes too thick, add a small splash of coconut milk and mix
Top with mixed nuts, berries, coconut flakes, and almond butter as desired

name: Power oats with protein powder
instructions: Put the oats, chia seeds, hemp seeds, ground flaxseed, water, and protein powder into a small saucepan
Heat for about 15 minutes over medium heat, stirring regularly
Add a splash of plant-based milk and let it cool slightly, or eat warm
Top with peanut butter, banana slices, berries, coconut flakes, and hemp seeds as desired
recipe_tips: Perfect for post-workout recovery with high protein and carbs
Stir regularly to prevent sticking
Adjust liquid amount to reach your preferred consistency
recipe_notes: High in fiber, protein, and omega-3 fatty acids. Excellent for recovery after solid workouts.

name: Marry Me Mushroom Pasta
instructions: Make cashew cream: Soak cashews in hot water for 30 minutes, then drain and blend with water until smooth and creamy
Slice king oyster mushroom stems lengthwise into thick slabs (about 1cm thick). Score the surface with a crosshatch pattern using a knife - this helps them absorb flavors
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Place mushroom slabs in the pan and DO NOT MOVE them for 3-4 minutes - this creates a golden, caramelized crust (the viral technique for meaty texture)
Flip mushrooms and sear the other side until golden brown. Remove from pan
In the same pan, sauté garlic until fragrant
Add sun-dried tomatoes and vegetable stock, let simmer briefly
Pour in the cashew cream and add nutritional yeast, stir to create a creamy sauce
Return mushrooms to the pan, simmer gently until everything is heated through
Serve over cooked pasta and garnish with fresh basil

Heat 1 tsp sesame oil in large wok over high heat
Cook tofu cubes until golden and crispy on all sides, about 4 minutes - remove and set aside
Add remaining sesame oil to wok, stir-fry bell pepper and carrots for 2 minutes
Add zucchini and sugar snaps, stir-fry 3 minutes more
Add minced garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds
Return crispy tofu to wok with vegetables
Whisk tahini, soy sauce, and rice vinegar in small bowl
Drain noodles and add to wok with vegetables and tofu
Pour tahini sauce over noodles and toss until coated
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve immediately

name: Creamy mushroom pasta
instructions: Make the sauce by placing cashews, almond milk, tamari, nutritional yeast, lemon juice and a sprinkling of salt into a powerful blender. Blitz to form a creamy sauce – you may wish to add a dash more almond milk, if needed
Place a pan over medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. Once warm, add the diced onion, garlic and a sprinkling of salt. Cook for 10 minutes until the onion is soft
Once soft, add the sliced mushrooms, dried rosemary and a sprinkling of salt. Mix well and cook for 10 minutes until the mushrooms reduce in size and soften
While the mushrooms cook, cook the pasta according to the packet instructions. Once cooked, drain (reserving a little of the pasta water) and mix through the mushroom mixture

Pour in the creamy sauce and add the spinach. Mix well and cook for a few minutes to heat everything through and allow the spinach to wilt; mix through a dash of the pasta water, if the sauce becomes too thick
Once everything has heated through, serve the pasta in bowls with some black pepper sprinkled on top
recipe_tips: Use a powerful blender to make the cashew sauce silky smooth
Reserve pasta water to adjust sauce consistency
Fresh rosemary can replace dried - use about 1 tsp fresh
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. This indulgent pasta recipe features a creamy cashew-based sauce with rosemary pan-fried mushrooms and spinach. It's a great recipe to make when you're short on time or low on ingredients

name: 10-minute butter beans with rocket pesto
ingredients: ½ lemon, zested & squeeze of juice
instructions: To make the beans, simply combine the butter beans, lemon zest, a good glug of olive oil, a pinch of flaky sea salt (taste the beans before adding salt, as they may be sufficiently seasoned), black pepper and a squeeze of lemon juice
For the pesto, add all the ingredients to a mini-chopper and blitz until you have a coarse paste, adding a splash of water to loosen as needed

To serve, tip the beans onto a large plate or shallow bowl, and spoon over the pesto, adding handful of rocket and a generous pinch of sumac (if using) to garnish. Delicious with fresh sourdough bread
recipe_tips: To batch cook or save leftovers, store the beans and pesto in separate sealed containers in the fridge for up to 3 days

recipe_tips: Keep heat high for proper stir-fry texture
Don't overcook broccoli - it should stay bright green
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Classic takeout flavors made keto-friendly and gluten-free

name: Protein pancakes with almond flour
ingredients: ½ cup almond gluten-free flour blend
instructions: Whisk eggs, protein powder, and Greek yogurt
Add almond flour, baking powder, vanilla, maple syrup
Let batter rest 5 minutes to thicken
Heat butter in non-stick pan over medium-low heat
Pour ¼ cup batter per pancake
Cook 2-3 minutes until bubbles form, flip carefully
Cook 1-2 minutes more until golden
Serve with fresh berries
recipe_tips: Keep heat medium-low to prevent burning
Batter will be thicker than regular pancakes
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Fluffy, protein-rich weekend breakfast treat

name: Gluten-free pasta with roasted vegetables and pesto
instructions: Cook chickpea or lentil pasta according to package instructions
Heat olive oil in large pan over medium-high heat
Add zucchini, broccoli and mushrooms, roast for 8-10 minutes
Add white beans and warm through for 2 minutes
Add cooked pasta to pan with vegetables
Stir in pesto and pine nuts
Add fresh spinach until wilted
Season with salt and pepper
recipe_tips: Don't overcook gluten-free pasta - check frequently
Toast pine nuts for extra flavor
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Naturally gluten-free and packed with colorful vegetables

name: Creamy gluten-free pasta carbonara with dairy-free sauce
instructions: Cook gluten-free pasta al dente, add peas in last 2 minutes
Cube and pan-fry vegetarian chicken pieces until golden
Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until soft
Add fresh spinach, cook until wilted (2 minutes)
Add coconut cream and nutritional yeast, stir until smooth
Toss hot pasta and peas with creamy sauce
Add protein pieces, crumbled vegan feta, and fresh parsley
Season generously with black pepper

benefits: Iron and vitamin K from greens

recipe_tips: Remove pan from heat when adding pasta to prevent curdling
Cook peas with pasta for convenience and perfect texture
Save pasta water to adjust sauce consistency
Add vegan feta just before serving for best texture
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Rich, creamy texture with added greens and vegan feta - balanced comfort food

name: Sticky tofu rice bowl with vegetables and kimchi
instructions: Pour rice into a small saucepan with 67ml water and a pinch of salt. Bring to boil, cover, reduce heat to low and simmer 10 minutes until water absorbed. Set aside
While rice cooks, warm 7ml olive oil in medium frying pan over medium-high heat. Add tofu and cook 10 minutes, turning occasionally, until crisp and golden
Add chilli oil and maple syrup to tofu, bubble 1-2 minutes to create sticky coating. Season with sea salt flakes, remove and set aside
Wipe pan clean, increase heat to high, add 5ml olive oil. Add garlic, ginger, and chilli, fry 3-4 minutes until golden (watch garlic doesn't burn)
Stir in cooked rice and place cabbage, kale, leek, and peas on top. Let sit 1-2 minutes without stirring
Add tamari and stir everything through, cook 3-5 minutes until vegetables just tender
Stir through sticky tofu, divide into bowl and add kimchi if using. Drizzle with any extra chilli sauce from tofu

benefits: Fermented foods from kimchi for gut health

dutch_name: Kleefrijst tofu kom met groenten en kimchi
dutch_instructions: Doe de rijst in een kleine steelpan met 67ml water en een snufje zout. Breng aan de kook, dek af, zet het vuur laag en laat 10 minuten sudderen tot het water is opgenomen. Zet apart.
Terwijl de rijst kookt, verwarm 7ml olijfolie in een koekenpan op middelhoog vuur. Voeg de tofu toe en bak 10 minuten, af en toe keren, tot knapperig en goudbruin.
Voeg chili-olie en ahornsiroop toe aan de tofu, laat 1-2 minuten borrelen voor een kleverige coating. Breng op smaak met zeezoutvlokken, verwijder en zet apart.
Veeg de pan schoon, verhoog het vuur naar hoog, voeg 5ml olijfolie toe. Voeg knoflook, gember en peper toe, bak 3-4 minuten tot goudbruin (let op dat knoflook niet verbrandt).
Roer de gekookte rijst erdoor en leg spitskool, boerenkool, prei en doperwten erop. Laat 1-2 minuten staan zonder roeren.
Voeg tamari toe en roer alles door, bak 3-5 minuten tot de groenten net gaar zijn.
Roer de kleeftofu erdoor, verdeel over een kom en voeg kimchi toe. Besprenkel met eventuele overgebleven chilisaus van de tofu.

name: Creamy mushroom pasta
ingredients: ½ tbsp extra virgin olive oil
¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Brush mushrooms clean and cut into large chunks
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add sliced onion and cook until translucent (3-4 minutes)
Add mushrooms and cook for 5-7 minutes until golden brown
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute
Add garlic granules, chili flakes, dried parsley, and oregano - stir well
Pour in white wine or water and simmer for 5 minutes until slightly reduced
Stir in dairy cream and simmer gently for 5-10 minutes until sauce thickens
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Toss cooked pasta with the creamy mushroom sauce
Top with chopped fresh parsley and serve

benefits: Rich in B vitamins from mushrooms

recipe_tips: Use a mix of mushroom varieties for deeper flavor (portobello, chestnut, shiitake)
Don't wash mushrooms - brush them clean to prevent sogginess
Let mushrooms brown without stirring too much for best flavor
Pasta geveltjes (gable-shaped pasta) works perfectly for catching the sauce
Add a splash of pasta water if sauce becomes too thick
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Rich and creamy mushroom pasta with white wine, garlic, and chili flakes. Classic Italian comfort food ready in 25 minutes.

name: Gluten-free creamy mushroom pasta with lactose-free cream
ingredients: 60-70g gluten-free short gluten-free pasta (rice or chickpea)
½ tbsp extra virgin olive oil
¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes
instructions: Cook gluten-free pasta according to package directions (usually 1-2 minutes longer than regular pasta), drain and set aside
Brush mushrooms clean and cut into large chunks
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add sliced onion and cook until translucent (3-4 minutes)
Add mushrooms and cook for 5-7 minutes until golden brown
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute
Add garlic granules, chili flakes, dried parsley, and oregano - stir well
Pour in white wine or water and simmer for 5 minutes until slightly reduced
Stir in lactose-free cream and simmer gently for 5-10 minutes until sauce thickens
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Toss cooked gluten-free pasta with the creamy mushroom sauce
Top with chopped fresh parsley and serve

benefits: Rich in B vitamins from mushrooms

recipe_tips: Gluten-free pasta can become mushy quickly - watch cooking time carefully
Rinse gluten-free pasta after cooking to remove excess starch
Lactose-free cream or oat cream creates the same creamy texture
Use a mix of mushroom varieties for deeper flavor
Let mushrooms brown without stirring too much for best flavor
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Gluten-free and lactose-free version of creamy mushroom pasta. Perfect for sensitive digestion while maintaining rich Italian flavor.

name: Creamy mushroom pasta with lactose-free cream
ingredients: ½ tbsp extra virgin olive oil
¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Brush mushrooms clean and cut into large chunks

Add sliced onion and cook until translucent (3-4 minutes)

name: Greek orzo salad with avocado and feta
instructions: Boil orzo in salted water for 8-10 minutes until al dente
Drain orzo, rinse lightly with cold water, and let cool slightly
In a bowl, mix diced cucumber, tomato, red onion, and halved olives
Add the cooled orzo and gently toss with the vegetables
Fold in diced avocado and crumbled feta cheese
Drizzle with olive oil and fresh lemon juice
Sprinkle with oregano, salt, and pepper
Toss gently to combine
Top with fresh parsley and serve immediately or chilled
recipe_tips: Don't overcook the orzo - it should be al dente for best texture
Add avocado just before serving to prevent browning
Can be made ahead without avocado and added fresh
Tastes even better after 30 minutes in the fridge
Double the recipe for easy meal prep
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Fresh Greek-inspired orzo salad with creamy avocado, tangy feta, and Mediterranean vegetables. Perfect light lunch ready in 20 minutes.

name: Gluten-free lactose-free Greek quinoa orzo salad with avocado
ingredients: 60g quinoa orzo gluten-free pasta (rice or chickpea)
instructions: Cook quinoa orzo according to package directions (typically 8-12 minutes, check package as gluten-free pasta varies)
Drain quinoa orzo, rinse lightly with cold water, and let cool slightly
In a bowl, mix diced cucumber, tomato, red onion, and halved olives
Add the cooled quinoa orzo and gently toss with the vegetables
Fold in diced avocado and crumbled lactose-free feta cheese
Drizzle with olive oil and fresh lemon juice
Sprinkle with oregano, salt, and pepper
Toss gently to combine
Top with fresh parsley and serve immediately or chilled

name: Mediterranean gluten-free pasta salad with olives
instructions: Cook pasta and rinse with cold water
Halve tomatoes and dice cucumber
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and salt
Combine pasta, vegetables, and olives
Add dressing and toss well
Crumble in dairy-free feta
Garnish with fresh basil before serving
recipe_tips: Chill for 30 minutes before serving for best flavor
Add extra lemon juice if pasta absorbs dressing
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Perfect make-ahead lunch, flavors improve over time

name: Rustic Tomato Pasta Bake with Herbed Breadcrumbs
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C fan / 390°F, grill setting
Make breadcrumbs by pulsing bread in blender until coarse crumbs form
Heat 30ml olive oil in large pan over medium heat
Add diced onion, garlic, and sun-dried tomatoes with pinch of salt
Cook 5-10 minutes until onions soften, stirring through oregano
Add tomato paste, canned tomatoes, and balsamic vinegar
Mix well and simmer 10-15 minutes until sauce thickens
Meanwhile, cook pasta according to package instructions and drain
For white sauce: heat remaining oil and flour in medium pan
Whisk to form smooth paste, cook 2-3 minutes over medium-high heat
Gradually stream in oat milk, whisking constantly until smooth and thick
Add nutritional yeast, mustard, crumbled tofu, salt and pepper
Combine pasta with tomato sauce in baking dish
Spoon white sauce over top and scatter with breadcrumbs
Bake 5-10 minutes until golden and bubbling
recipe_tips: Use good-quality sourdough for best breadcrumb texture
Don't let the flour burn when making the white sauce
Crumble tofu finely for better integration
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. A comforting pasta bake with rich tomato sauce and creamy protein-packed white sauce

name: Rustic Tomato Pasta Bake with Herbed Breadcrumbs
ingredients: 400g gluten-free penne gluten-free pasta (rice or chickpea)
40ml rice gluten-free flour blend + 15ml tapioca starch + 5ml xanthan gum
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C fan / 390°F, grill setting
Make breadcrumbs by pulsing gluten-free bread in blender until coarse crumbs form
Heat 30ml olive oil in large pan over medium heat
Add diced onion, garlic, and sun-dried tomatoes with pinch of salt
Cook 5-10 minutes until onions soften, stirring through oregano
Add tomato paste, canned tomatoes, and balsamic vinegar
Mix well and simmer 10-15 minutes until sauce thickens
Meanwhile, cook gluten-free pasta according to package instructions and drain
For white sauce: heat remaining oil and rice flour mixture in medium pan
Whisk to form smooth paste, cook 2-3 minutes over medium-high heat (rice flour creates better texture)
Gradually stream in oat milk, whisking constantly until smooth and thick
Add nutritional yeast, mustard, cooked lentils, hemp hearts, salt and pepper
Combine pasta with tomato sauce in baking dish
Spoon white sauce over top and scatter with breadcrumbs
Bake 5-10 minutes until golden and bubbling
recipe_tips: Use certified gluten-free bread for safe breadcrumbs
Rice flour + tapioca starch + xanthan gum creates better texture than single flour
Pre-cooked lentils work best - canned or leftover home-cooked
Hemp hearts add extra protein and nutty flavor
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Gluten-free version using rice flour blend with xanthan gum for optimal texture, plus lentils and hemp hearts for extra plant protein

name: Chickpea & Potato Pancakes with Warm Veggie Salsa
ingredients: Half large red onion, finely chopped
instructions: Make pancake batter by whisking gram flour, 5ml olive oil, and salt with 60ml water until combined with no lumps
Set batter aside to rest
Cut potatoes into 1cm pieces
Heat generous drizzle of olive oil in non-stick frying pan over medium heat
Add potato pieces with pinch of salt, pan-fry stirring frequently until golden brown and cooked through (12-15 minutes). Tip: Cover pan for first 5 minutes to help potatoes steam and cook faster
Transfer cooked potatoes to plate
Return pan to heat with small drizzle of oil
Spoon pancake batter into pan - make 2 medium pancakes or 1 large
Sprinkle cooked potato pieces on top
Cook until golden brown on both sides (about 2 minutes per side, add 1-2 minutes if making 1 large pancake)
Transfer pancakes to serving plate
Add another drizzle of oil to pan and add onion, pepper, and tomatoes
Cook until hot and juicy (about 3 minutes)
Stir in spinach and cook until wilted (1-2 minutes)
Season veggie salsa with salt and pepper
Spoon warm veggie salsa on top of pancakes and sprinkle with hemp hearts
Serve immediately

name: Vegetarian Rice Paper Rolls with Marinated Shiitake & Fresh Herbs
ingredients: Fresh cilantro, mint, and Thai basil leaves
Marinade: soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, ginger, garlic
instructions: Make marinade: whisk 45ml soy sauce, 15ml sesame oil, 15ml rice vinegar, 10ml grated ginger, 2 cloves garlic (minced)
Cube tofu and slice shiitake mushrooms, marinate both in half the marinade for 20 minutes
Sauté marinated shiitakes until golden and caramelized, about 8 minutes
Pan-fry marinated tofu cubes until golden on all sides
Cook edamame according to package directions, cool slightly
Make tahini sauce: mix tahini with remaining marinade and 30ml water until smooth
Prepare vegetables: blanch sugar snap peas, slice bell pepper, rinse bean sprouts, slice avocado
Pick fresh herb leaves from cilantro, mint, and Thai basil
Soak rice papers in warm water until pliable
Layer half nori sheet, then marinated tofu, caramelized shiitakes, edamame, and vegetables
Sprinkle hemp hearts, add fresh herbs and avocado slices
Drizzle with tahini sauce before rolling tightly
Slice diagonally and serve with extra tahini dipping sauce
recipe_tips: Marinated shiitakes provide deep umami that rivals any meat
Press tofu well before marinating for better texture
Hemp hearts add protein and omega-3s without changing flavor
Edamame and tahini boost the protein to 24g per serving
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. The combination of marinated shiitakes, tofu, edamame, and tahini creates a protein powerhouse while fresh herbs provide bright Asian flavors. Much more satisfying than typical vegetarian rolls.

name: Gluten-Free Crispy Aubergine with Spiced Tomato Sauce & Almond Coating
instructions: Slice aubergine lengthwise into 1cm thick planks, salt generously and let drain 20 minutes
Meanwhile, make spiced tomato sauce: heat olive oil, sauté shallot until soft
Add garlic, cook 1 minute, then add crushed tomatoes, sugar, salt, pepper
Simmer sauce 15 minutes until thickened, stir in fresh basil at end
Pat aubergine slices dry with paper towels
Make gluten-free flour blend: mix rice flour, tapioca starch, and xanthan gum
Set up breading station: flour blend seasoned with salt and pepper, plant-based milk, panko mixed with ground almonds, hemp hearts and nutritional yeast
Dredge aubergine: flour blend, then milk, then almond-panko mixture, pressing gently
Season with paprika, oregano, and chili flakes before cooking
Pan method: heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat, fry breaded slices 3-4 minutes per side until golden and crispy
Air fryer method: preheat air fryer to 200°C, brush slices lightly with olive oil, air fry 12-15 minutes flipping halfway, until golden and crispy
Serve immediately over warm spiced tomato sauce, garnish with fresh basil
recipe_tips: Rice flour + tapioca starch + xanthan gum creates perfect gluten-free binding
Ground almonds add richness and protein while maintaining crispiness
Lower frying temperature prevents almond coating from burning
Tapioca starch gives the light, crispy texture missing in most gluten-free coatings
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. This gluten-free version uses a carefully balanced flour blend that delivers the same satisfying crunch as wheat. Ground almonds boost both protein and flavor complexity while hemp hearts maintain the nutritional profile.

name: Vegan Jackfruit Rendang with Cashew Protein & Coconut Rice
ingredients: 2 cans young green jackfruit in brine, drained
instructions: Drain and shred jackfruit into pulled meat-like strands
Soak cashews in warm water for 20 minutes for protein blend
Make spice paste: blend soaked chilies, shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, lemongrass, and macadamias
Cook jasmine rice with 200ml coconut milk for fragrant coconut rice
Heat coconut oil in heavy pot, fry spice paste 8-10 minutes until dark and fragrant
Add shredded jackfruit, stir-fry 5 minutes to absorb spices
Blend soaked cashews with 100ml water until smooth and creamy
Add coconut milk, coconut cream, cashew cream, tamarind, coconut sugar
Add ground spices and torn kaffir lime leaves
Simmer uncovered 1-1.5 hours, stirring regularly, until sauce reduces
In final 20 minutes, add hemp hearts and nutritional yeast for extra protein
Cook until sauce is thick and coating jackfruit pieces
Adjust seasoning with salt, coconut sugar, and tamarind
Serve over coconut rice, garnish with crushed toasted cashews
recipe_tips: Jackfruit texture improves with longer cooking - becomes more meat-like
Cashew cream adds richness and significant protein boost
Hemp hearts dissolve into sauce while adding omega-3s and protein
Toast cashews separately for textural contrast
recipe_notes: This vegan version achieves 24g protein through clever combinations of jackfruit, cashew cream, hemp hearts, and nutritional yeast. The slow-cooked jackfruit develops incredible texture that rivals traditional beef rendang.

name: Crispy Herb-Crusted Chicken with Spiced Tomato Sauce
instructions: Cut chicken breast into bite-sized chunks, season with salt and pepper
Make spiced tomato sauce: heat olive oil, sauté chopped shallots until soft
Add minced garlic, cook 1 minute, then add crushed tomatoes, sugar, salt, pepper
Simmer sauce 15 minutes until thickened, stir in fresh basil at end
Set up breading station: seasoned flour, milk, panko with paprika and oregano
Dredge chicken chunks: flour, then milk, then seasoned panko, pressing gently
Heat olive oil in large pan over medium-high heat
Fry breaded chicken pieces 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through
Check internal temperature reaches 75°C
Season with extra paprika and oregano while hot
Serve immediately over warm spiced tomato sauce with fresh basil
recipe_tips: Cut chicken evenly for consistent cooking
Don't overcrowd pan - fry in batches if needed
Press panko coating gently to help it stick
Fresh basil added at end preserves bright flavor
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Crispy herb-crusted chicken with vibrant tomato sauce creates a satisfying comfort food that delivers excellent protein while maintaining indulgent flavors.

name: Earl Grey Tea-Infused Quinoa Porridge with Caramelized Persimmon
instructions: Prepare Earl Grey tea: Brew 2 strong tea bags in 200ml boiling water for 5 minutes, then remove bags and cool completely
Cook quinoa ahead with tea: Rinse 200g quinoa and simmer in 300ml Earl Grey tea + 100ml water for 15 minutes until tender. Store in fridge for up to 5 days
In medium saucepan, combine oats, 80g tea-infused pre-cooked quinoa, oat milk, remaining Earl Grey tea, and pinch of salt
Bring to gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally
Meanwhile, heat small frying pan over medium heat
Add diced persimmon with half the maple syrup and cinnamon
Cook persimmon gently for 6-8 minutes until caramelized and jammy
When porridge is creamy and Earl Grey-scented, remove from heat
Stir in protein powder, vanilla extract, ginger, and cardamom until smooth
Add hemp hearts and remaining maple syrup, mix well
Serve in warm bowl topped with caramelized persimmon
Sprinkle with chopped walnuts, pumpkin seeds, and extra cinnamon
recipe_tips: Earl Grey's bergamot adds elegant citrus notes that pair beautifully with persimmon
If persimmons aren't available, use very ripe pears for similar sweetness
Tea-infused quinoa can be batch-cooked and adds sophisticated flavor to the porridge
recipe_notes: Elegant breakfast porridge infused with Earl Grey tea's bergamot essence, topped with caramelized persimmon for natural sweetness and winter warmth

name: Caramelized Fig & Pistachio Breakfast Bowl
instructions: Begin by slicing the figs in half (or quarters if you prefer larger pieces)
Heat a medium frying pan over medium-low heat
Melt the butter and cinnamon in the pan, stirring to combine
Drizzle the honey into the butter mixture and stir with a wooden spoon
Gently place the fig halves cut-side down in the pan
Leave for 3-4 minutes to slowly caramelize without stirring
Once the figs are nicely soft and jammy, remove from pan and set aside on a plate
Using the same pan with the remaining juices, add the oats and pistachios
Stir continuously and cook for 2-3 minutes until the oats have toasted and browned nicely
Place the Greek yogurt in a serving bowl
Top the yogurt with the toasted oat and pistachio mixture
Arrange the caramelized figs on top of the granola mix
Drizzle with extra honey if desired and serve immediately
recipe_benefits: High fiber content supports digestive health
Natural sugars from figs provide sustained energy
Pistachios offer healthy fats and protein
Cinnamon helps regulate blood sugar
Probiotics from yogurt support gut health
Seasonal autumn fruit provides antioxidants
recipe_tips: Choose figs that are ripe but still firm - they'll caramelize better without falling apart
Don't move the figs while caramelizing to get a beautiful golden color
Save the pan juices to toast the oats - they add incredible flavor
This recipe works beautifully with any type of yogurt you prefer
recipe_notes: A stunning autumn breakfast that celebrates fresh figs at their peak, with warm spices and crunchy pistachios creating the perfect seasonal start to your day

name: Tomato Mac & Cheese
instructions: Cook the macaroni pasta according to packet instructions until al dente
Once cooked, drain pasta and set aside until needed
Make the white sauce: place tapioca flour, lemon juice, nutritional yeast, Dijon mustard, almond milk and a pinch of salt in a pan over medium heat
Continuously whisk the mixture to ensure it stays smooth and no lumps form
Add extra splash of almond milk if the mixture becomes too thick
In another large pan over medium heat, add olive oil
Once warm, add diced garlic, halved cherry tomatoes and a sprinkling of salt
Mix well and cook for 10 minutes until the tomatoes become really soft and begin to bubble
Mix through the drained pasta, white sauce and chopped basil
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve immediately
recipe_tips: Don't overcook the pasta as it will continue cooking when mixed with the hot sauce
Whisk the white sauce constantly to prevent lumps from forming
Cook tomatoes until they're really soft and jammy for best flavor
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Comforting vegan mac and cheese with sweet cherry tomatoes and creamy nutritional yeast sauce - perfect for pasta lovers

name: Harissa-Roasted Vegetable Pancakes with Chickpeas
instructions: Preheat oven to 170°C fan
Cut half aubergine into bite-sized chunks
Arrange drained chickpeas and aubergine chunks on baking tray
Add 1g paprika, drizzle 5ml olive oil, and pinch of salt to vegetables
Mix well and cook for 25 minutes
Cut each tomato into 6 pieces
Add tomato pieces and cook another 10-15 minutes until aubergine is soft
Remove vegetables from oven and transfer to bowl
Stir through harissa paste, dash of plant-based milk, chopped parsley, tahini, lemon juice, and 1g salt
Mix vegetable mixture until everything comes together
For pancake: combine gluten-free flour, chilli powder, 1g paprika, 1g salt, and pepper in bowl
Mix flour mixture well to remove lumps
Pour in 70ml water and mix to form thick batter
Heat frying pan over medium heat with 5ml olive oil
Pour in the batter and cook 5 minutes until bottom is set
Flip pancake with spatula and cook 2-3 minutes until cooked through
Serve pancake topped with harissa-roasted vegetables
Garnish with fresh parsley and pinch of chilli flakes
recipe_tips: Use certified gluten-free gram flour for complete celiac safety
Adjust harissa paste amount to taste preference
Vegetables can be roasted in advance and reheated
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Naturally gluten-free pancakes with Mediterranean flavors and plant-based protein from chickpeas and gram flour

name: Protein Churro Bites
instructions: Mix oat flour, protein powder, cinnamon, baking powder and salt in a bowl
In separate bowl, whisk almond butter, almond milk, maple syrup and vanilla
Combine wet and dry ingredients until smooth dough forms
Let rest 10 minutes for oat flour to hydrate
Roll dough into 8 small balls, then roll into finger shapes
Heat coconut oil in non-stick pan over medium heat
Cook churro bites 2-3 minutes per side until golden
Dust with extra cinnamon while warm
recipe_tips: Don't overmix the dough to keep texture light
Make sure pan isn't too hot to prevent burning
Store leftovers in airtight container for 2 days
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Satisfies sweet cravings while supporting blood sugar stability and providing protein for muscle recovery

name: Aggie's Dreamy Avocado Brownies
instructions: Preheat oven to 175°C fan/340°F
Line a small baking dish with parchment paper
Mash avocado until completely smooth
Mix in maple syrup, vanilla extract and protein powder
In separate bowl, whisk cocoa powder, almond flour, baking powder and salt
Fold dry ingredients into avocado mixture until just combined
Fold in chocolate chips gently
Pour into prepared dish and smooth top
Bake 22-25 minutes until set but still fudgy
Cool completely before cutting
recipe_tips: Use very ripe avocado for best texture and sweetness
Don't overbake - brownies should still be slightly soft in center
Chill for 2 hours for fudgiest texture
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Rich in magnesium for muscle relaxation and mood support, with healthy fats that reduce inflammation during menstruation

name: Breakfast Muffins
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C fan/375°F
Grease 6-cup muffin tin or line with cases
Beat eggs in large bowl, whisk in milk and olive oil
Add flour, salt and pepper, mix until just combined
Fold in grated cheese and chopped vegetables
Divide mixture between muffin cases
Bake 18-20 minutes until golden and set
Cool in tin 5 minutes before turning out
recipe_tips: Don't overmix batter to keep muffins light

Mix gently with hands until just combined

instructions: Blend oats in blender until flour-like consistency

name: Garlic Butter Chicken
instructions: Season chicken breast with salt and pepper
Heat 1 tbsp butter in pan over medium-high heat

instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C for sweet potatoes

ingredients: 5g almond gluten-free flour blend or oat flour
instructions: Preheat oven to 163°C (325°F)
Add all ingredients (cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, egg, pistachios, maple syrup, almond flour, and vanilla) to a blender
Blend on high speed until completely smooth with no pistachio chunks remaining
Pour the mixture into a small ramekin or individual baking dish (about 10cm/4 inches) lined with parchment paper
Bake for 15-20 minutes. The edges should feel set but the center should still have a slight jiggle when you move the dish
Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature
Transfer to the fridge and chill for at least 2-4 hours (overnight is best)
Serve chilled, optionally topped with extra crushed pistachios

benefits: High protein from dairy and eggs

recipe_benefits: 18g protein from cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, and eggs for muscle support
Pistachios provide heart-healthy fats and antioxidants
Make-ahead breakfast option - prep the night before
Creamy, indulgent texture with natural sweetness
Individual portion perfect for meal prep
recipe_tips: The center will set completely as it cools in the fridge - don't overbake
For a sweeter cheesecake, add an extra tablespoon of maple syrup
Line your ramekin with parchment paper for easy removal
Store covered in the fridge for up to 3 days
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Individual high-protein pistachio cheesecake that's perfect for meal prep breakfasts. The creamy texture and natural pistachio flavor make this feel like dessert while providing substantial protein to start your day.
