name: Lactose-free protein cookie dough bites
instructions: In a bowl, mix almond flour, protein powder, and salt
Add almond butter and maple syrup, mix until crumbly
Add almond milk 1 tablespoon at a time until dough forms
Fold in lactose-free chocolate chips
Roll into 4 equal-sized balls
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before eating
Store in airtight container in fridge for up to 1 week

benefits: High protein from nuts and protein powder

recipe_benefits: 24g protein per serving supports muscle maintenance
No-bake recipe perfect for meal prep
Satisfies sweet tooth while providing nutrition
Portable snack for on-the-go
recipe_tips: Dough should be slightly sticky but hold together - add more almond milk if too dry
Use certified lactose-free chocolate chips for completely dairy-free
Roll in cocoa powder or coconut for extra flavor
Double the batch for meal prep
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Viral no-bake cookie dough bites that are safe to eat and lactose-free. Perfect high-protein snack for anyone avoiding dairy.

name: Marry me butter chicken pasta
instructions: Cook whole wheat pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, cook chicken until golden and cooked through (6-8 minutes)
Remove chicken, add minced garlic to pan, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Return chicken to pan, mix well
Top with grated parmesan and serve immediately
recipe_benefits: 42g protein from chicken and dairy for muscle support
Whole wheat pasta provides sustained energy
Greek yogurt adds creaminess and probiotics
Sun-dried tomatoes boost flavor and antioxidants
Viral 'marry me' flavor that actually delivers nutrition
recipe_tips: Reserve pasta water to thin sauce if needed
Use full-fat Greek yogurt for creamiest texture
Butterfly chicken breast for faster cooking
Fresh spinach adds color and nutrition without overwhelming flavor
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Viral 'marry me' pasta combining butter chicken flavors with Italian comfort food. This protein-packed version keeps the indulgent taste while delivering serious nutrition.

name: Gluten-free lactose-free marry me butter chicken pasta
instructions: Cook gluten-free pasta according to package directions (usually 1-2 minutes less than wheat pasta), drain and set aside
Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, cook chicken until golden and cooked through (6-8 minutes)
Remove chicken, add minced garlic to pan, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in lactose-free Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Return chicken to pan, mix well and serve immediately
recipe_benefits: All the viral flavor, none of the digestive issues
Sun-dried tomatoes add depth and antioxidants
recipe_tips: Gluten-free pasta can get mushy - cook al dente
Use certified gluten-free spice blends to avoid cross-contamination
Lactose-free yogurt behaves exactly like regular in this recipe
Reserve extra pasta water - gluten-free pasta absorbs more liquid
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Gluten-free and lactose-free version of the viral 'marry me' pasta. Perfect for those with celiac disease or lactose intolerance who don't want to miss out on trending recipes.

name: Vegetarian marry me mushroom pasta
instructions: Cook whole wheat pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Slice mushrooms, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, sauté mushrooms until golden and liquid evaporates (5-6 minutes)
Add drained chickpeas to pan, cook 2 minutes until slightly crispy
Add minced garlic, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Top with grated parmesan and serve immediately
recipe_benefits: 28g plant-based protein from chickpeas, dairy, and mushrooms
Mushrooms provide umami depth replacing chicken
Chickpeas add protein and fiber for satiety
Vegetarian version of viral recipe without compromise
Rich in B vitamins and minerals
recipe_tips: Don't crowd mushrooms in pan - cook in batches for best browning
Use a mix of mushroom varieties for complex flavor
Chickpeas add protein and creamy texture when mashed slightly
Fresh herbs like cilantro make great garnish
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Vegetarian version of viral 'marry me' pasta using hearty mushrooms and protein-rich chickpeas. All the flavor and creaminess of the original without the meat.

name: Lactose-free protein churro breakfast bowl
instructions: In a bowl, mix lactose-free Greek yogurt with vanilla protein powder until smooth
Stir in cinnamon until evenly distributed
Transfer to serving bowl
Drizzle with honey
Top with granola for crunch
Add sliced fresh strawberries
Finish with lactose-free dark chocolate shavings
Serve immediately
recipe_benefits: Tastes like churros but delivers breakfast nutrition
Cinnamon helps regulate blood sugar
Ready in 12 minutes - perfect for busy mornings
recipe_tips: Mix protein powder thoroughly to avoid lumps
Use warm cinnamon-toasted granola for extra churro flavor
Fresh berries can be swapped for any seasonal fruit
Make it vegan by using plant-based yogurt and protein powder
Drizzle with almond butter for extra protein and healthy fats
recipe_notes: Viral churro bowl that tastes like dessert for breakfast. Lactose-free version is gentle on digestion while maintaining the creamy, sweet churro flavor everyone loves.

name: Lactose-free cucumber sushi protein boats
instructions: Cut cucumber in half lengthwise, scoop out seeds with spoon to create 'boats'
Cut each half into 3 equal pieces (6 boats total)
In bowl, mix drained tuna, lactose-free Greek yogurt, and sriracha
Fill each cucumber boat with tuna mixture
Top with thin avocado slices
Sprinkle with sesame seeds
Garnish with nori seaweed strips
Serve with soy sauce on the side
recipe_benefits: 24g protein from tuna with minimal calories
Low-carb alternative to sushi rice
Visually stunning presentation perfect for social media
Refreshing and light - ideal for summer snacking
recipe_tips: Pat cucumber dry after scooping to prevent soggy filling
Use a melon baller to easily remove cucumber seeds
Adjust sriracha to your spice preference
Make ahead and store in fridge for up to 4 hours
Swap tuna for cooked shrimp or salmon for variety
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Viral cucumber sushi boats that are low-carb, high-protein, and visually stunning. Lactose-free version uses yogurt instead of mayo for creamy texture.

name: Healthy sweet potato brownies
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C/390°F
Use a fork to poke a few holes into sweet potato, bake with skin on until tender (about 30-40 minutes)
Let the sweet potato cool down completely
Peel the sweet potato and add to a bowl with peanut butter, mix well until smooth
Add cacao powder, oat flour, salt, baking powder, vanilla, and sweetener - mix well
Add cold espresso shot and plant milk, mix until combined
Fold in chopped dark chocolate (make sure espresso is cold so chocolate doesn't melt)
Pour batter into a greased oven-proof dish (20x20cm recommended)
Reduce oven temperature to 176°C/350°F
Bake for 35-40 minutes until set but still fudgy in the center
Let cool completely before cutting into 12 squares
recipe_benefits: Sweet potato provides natural sweetness and moisture
Rich in antioxidants from cacao and dark chocolate
High fiber content supports digestive health
Peanut butter adds healthy fats and protein
Makes 12 brownies - perfect for meal prep
recipe_tips: Bake sweet potato ahead of time to save prep time
If you don't have oat flour, grind rolled oats in a blender
Espresso shot MUST be cold or chocolate will melt into the batter
For sweeter brownies, increase sweetener or use maple syrup
Store in airtight container for up to 5 days
Can be frozen for up to 3 months
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Viral sweet potato brownies that are incredibly rich and gooey. These taste like the real deal but are packed with whole food ingredients. Perfect healthy snack for meal prep!

name: Cacao-blueberry power oats
instructions: In a pot, combine oats, buckwheat flakes, and almond milk
Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes until creamy
Remove from heat and stir in cacao powder, protein powder, cinnamon, and salt
Mix until protein powder is fully incorporated
Transfer to bowl
Top with fresh blueberries and drizzle with almond butter
Serve immediately

benefits: High in antioxidants from cacao and blueberries

recipe_benefits: 85mg cocoa flavanols from cacao powder and blueberries
High in protein and fiber for sustained energy
Magnesium from cacao supports muscle function
Antioxidants support cardiovascular health
recipe_tips: Use frozen blueberries if fresh aren't available
Add cacao powder after cooking to preserve flavanols
Adjust liquid to desired consistency
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Power-packed breakfast with 85mg flavanols supporting heart and brain health

name: Buckwheat pasta with roasted vegetables and cacao sauce
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Toss broccoli and bell peppers with olive oil, salt, and pepper
Roast vegetables for 20-25 minutes until tender and slightly charred
Cook buckwheat pasta according to package instructions
In saucepan, warm tomato sauce with minced garlic
Stir cacao powder into sauce for depth of flavor
Drain pasta and toss with cacao tomato sauce
Add roasted vegetables
Top with dark chocolate pieces (they'll melt slightly)
Garnish with fresh basil
Serve immediately
recipe_benefits: 26mg cocoa flavanols from cacao powder and dark chocolate
High fiber from buckwheat and vegetables
Antioxidants from tomatoes and cacao
recipe_tips: Cacao adds richness without sweetness
Use chickpea pasta for extra protein
Dark chocolate provides additional flavanols
Gluten-free products may need different cooking times
Check labels to ensure all ingredients are certified gluten-free
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using alternative grains and flours. Hearty pasta dish with 26mg flavanols from cacao and dark chocolate

name: Crispy kimchi tofu with kale and edamame salad
instructions: Press the tofu wrapped in a tea towel in 10 second bursts until no more visible water comes out. Cut into slabs and marinate in the kimchi juice for at least 10 minutes.
Prepare the quick pickled cucumber by chopping it into half moons and combining with apple cider vinegar and a pinch of sea salt. Set aside.
Blanch the kale by running boiling water over it in a sieve for 5 seconds, then rinse under cold water. Squeeze out moisture and chop finely.
Warm the wild rice and season with half the tamari. Combine with kale, edamame and pickled cucumber in a mixing bowl.
Make the tahini sauce by blending tahini, remaining lemon juice, miso paste, remaining tamari and water until smooth.
Mix chickpea flour with paprika powder, onion powder, garlic granules, salt and pepper. Put panko breadcrumbs on a separate plate.
Coat each tofu slab in the chickpea flour mix, dip back in kimchi juice, then press into panko breadcrumbs.
Heat sesame oil in a non-stick pan and fry tofu slabs for 1-2 minutes each side until golden brown.
Serve the salad in bowls, slice the crispy tofu diagonally and place on top. Drizzle with tahini sauce and sprinkle with sesame seeds and kimchi.
dutch_name: Krokante kimchi tofu met boerenkool en edamame salade
dutch_instructions: Pers de tofu gewikkeld in een theedoek in bursts van 10 seconden tot er geen zichtbaar water meer uitkomt. Snijd in plakken en marineer in het kimchi sap gedurende minimaal 10 minuten.
Bereid de snelgepickelde komkommer voor door deze in halve maantjes te snijden en te combineren met appelazijn en een snufje zeezout. Zet apart.
Blancheer de boerenkool door er 5 seconden kokend water overheen te gieten in een zeef, spoel dan af onder koud water. Knijp het vocht eruit en hak fijn.
Verwarm de wilde rijst en breng op smaak met de helft van de tamari. Combineer met boerenkool, edamame en gepickelde komkommer in een mengkom.
Maak de tahinisaus door tahini, resterende citroensap, miso pasta, resterende tamari en water glad te blenden.
Meng kikkererwtenmeel met gerookte paprika, uienpoeder, knoflookgranules, zout en peper. Doe het panko paneermeel op een apart bord.
Bedek elke tofu plak met het kikkererwtenmeelmengsel, doop terug in kimchi sap en druk dan in het panko paneermeel.
Verhit sesamolie in een antiaanbakpan en bak de tofu plakken 1-2 minuten per kant tot ze goudbruin zijn.
Serveer de salade in kommen, snijd de krokante tofu diagonaal en leg bovenop. Besprenkel met tahinisaus en bestrooi met sesamzaadjes en kimchi.

name: Pumpkin-coconut pasta
instructions: Rehydrate the dried soy mince in 100ml hot vegetable stock for 10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Peel the pumpkin (optional) and cut into small cubes. Finely chop the onion and garlic.
Boil the pumpkin cubes for 10 minutes until soft. Drain, return to the pan, and blend into a smooth purée with a stick blender.
Heat a frying pan with oil and cook the onion for 8 minutes. Add garlic, curry leaves, and rehydrated soy mince. Cook 3 minutes more. Set half of the curry leaves aside on kitchen paper for garnish.
Add coconut milk, pumpkin purée, stock cube, spices, olives, and lemon juice to the pan. Stir and simmer on low heat.
Cook the gluten-free pasta according to package instructions. Drain and mix with the sauce.
Serve topped with the reserved curry leaves and toasted pine nuts.
dutch_instructions: Peel the pumpkin (optional) and cut into small cubes. Finely chop the onion and garlic.
Boil the pumpkin cubes for 10 minutes until soft. Drain, return to the pan, and blend into a smooth purée with a stick blender.
Heat a frying pan with oil and cook the onion for 8 minutes. Add garlic and curry leaves and cook 2 minutes more. Set half of the curry leaves aside on kitchen paper for garnish.
Add coconut milk, pumpkin purée, stock cube, spices, olives, and lemon juice to the pan. Stir and simmer on low heat.
Cook the gluten-free pasta according to package instructions. Drain and mix with the sauce.
Serve topped with the reserved curry leaves and toasted pine nuts.

name: Sweet Potato and Bean Traybake with Quinoa and Tahini
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C
Cook 80g quinoa according to package instructions and set aside
Add the sweet potato, mushrooms, red onion, paprika, oregano, 5ml olive oil, 1g salt and black pepper to a baking tray and toss together
Wrap 2 garlic cloves (unpeeled) in foil with 2ml olive oil and tuck into corner of baking tray
Place the tray in the oven and cook for 30 minutes, stirring halfway, until golden
Remove the tray from the oven, then stir in the black beans, butter beans, cherry tomatoes and ginger
Return to the oven for 15-20 minutes, until the tomato skins begin to burst
For the garlic yoghurt: carefully squeeze the flesh of the roasted garlic into a small bowl
Stir the garlic into the coconut yoghurt and add 0.5g salt
Stir the cooked quinoa into the roasted vegetables
Top with lime juice, chilli flakes, fresh coriander, tahini drizzle and 1g sea salt
Serve with the roasted garlic yoghurt on the side

name: Gluten-free pasta e fagioli with walnut pesto
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a large, deep saucepan set over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the onion, carrot and a pinch of salt. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened but not coloured.
Add the tomatoes, cannellini beans, miso paste and vegetable stock. Simmer for 20 minutes until slightly thickened and the carrots are tender.
Add the gluten-free pasta and cavolo nero. Cook for 10-12 minutes until the pasta is al dente.
Meanwhile, make the pesto: blend the parsley, walnuts, garlic, olive oil, chilli flakes and salt in a food processor until smooth.
Serve the pasta e fagioli in a bowl, topped with a spoonful of walnut pesto and a drizzle of olive oil.
dutch_name: Glutenvrije pasta e fagioli met walnotenpesto
dutch_instructions: Verwarm de olijfolie in een grote, diepe steelpan op middelhoog vuur. Voeg de knoflook toe en bak 1-2 minuten tot geurig.
Voeg de ui, wortel en een snufje zout toe. Bak 10 minuten, af en toe roerend, tot zacht maar niet gekleurd.
Voeg de tomaten, cannellinibonen, misopasta en groentebouillon toe. Laat 20 minuten sudderen tot licht ingedikt en de wortels zacht zijn.
Voeg de glutenvrije pasta en cavolo nero toe. Kook 10-12 minuten tot de pasta al dente is.
Maak ondertussen de pesto: blend de peterselie, walnoten, knoflook, olijfolie, chilivlokken en zout in een keukenmachine tot glad.
Serveer de pasta e fagioli in een kom, afgewerkt met een lepel walnotenpesto en een scheut olijfolie.

name: Lactose-free matcha-raspberry chocolate bar
instructions: Line a small tray or plate with baking paper.
If using frozen raspberries, let them sit 5 minutes. Spread raspberries into a tight rectangle about 1-1.5 cm thick. Press gently so berries connect.
Freeze the raspberry layer for 20-30 minutes until firm.
Mix coconut yogurt, matcha powder, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and salt until smooth. Taste and adjust sweetness if needed.
Spread matcha yogurt over the frozen raspberries. Return to freezer for 30-45 minutes until solid.
Melt dark chocolate with coconut oil in short microwave bursts or au bain-marie.
Pour or drizzle chocolate over the frozen matcha layer. Tap tray to level.
Freeze 10-15 minutes until chocolate sets.
Break or slice into bars. Keep frozen for clean pieces. Eat straight from freezer or let sit 2-3 minutes for a softer bite.
dutch_instructions: Bekleed een klein dienblad of bord met bakpapier.
Als je bevroren frambozen gebruikt, laat ze 5 minuten staan. Verdeel de frambozen in een strakke rechthoek van ongeveer 1-1.5 cm dik. Druk zachtjes aan zodat de bessen met elkaar verbinden.
Vries de frambozenlaag 20-30 minuten tot stevig.
Meng kokosyoghurt, matchapoeder, ahornsiroop, vanille-extract en zout tot glad. Proef en pas de zoetheid aan indien nodig.
Smeer de matcha-yoghurt over de bevroren frambozen. Zet terug in de vriezer voor 30-45 minuten tot vast.
Smelt pure chocolade met kokosolie in korte magnetronintervallen of au bain-marie.
Giet of druppel de chocolade over de bevroren matchalaag. Tik op het dienblad om te egaliseren.
Vries 10-15 minuten tot de chocolade is uitgehard.
Breek of snijd in repen. Houd bevroren voor schone stukken. Eet direct uit de vriezer of laat 2-3 minuten staan voor een zachtere hap.

name: Coconut lentil dal with cavolo nero
instructions: Peel and finely chop the garlic and ginger. Finely chop the onion. Drain and rinse the tinned lentils thoroughly.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large saucepan over high heat. Add the garlic, ginger, and onion, and cook for 1 minute, stirring regularly.
Add the lentils, cavolo nero, coconut milk, lemon juice, curry powder, cumin, pepper, salt, tamari, and maple syrup.
Bring to the boil, stirring occasionally. Reduce to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes until heated through and creamy.
Remove from heat. Serve garnished with roasted pumpkin, pickled red onion, fresh coriander, or chilli flakes. Serve with warm pitta bread or rice.

name: Orange and olive oil cake (gluten-free vegan)
ingredients: 1 large orange, thinly sliced for topping
instructions: Preheat your oven to 160°C (fan)
Line a loaf tin with baking paper
Pour a thin layer of simple syrup into the base of the tin
Lay the orange slices over the syrup in a neat, slightly overlapping layer
Mix the dry ingredients (flour, sugar, baking powder) in a large bowl
Mix the wet ingredients (plant-based milk, orange juice, olive oil, orange zest, vanilla) in a separate bowl
Combine wet and dry mixtures. Stir until just combined (don't overmix)
Pour batter gently over the orange slices in the tin and smooth the top
Bake for 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean
Cool slightly, then flip the cake out of the tin to serve with the orange slices on top

name: Indonesian-style mash
instructions: Boil potatoes in salted water until tender (15–20 min). Reserve some cooking water; drain.
Return potatoes to the pot to steam-dry 1 min. Add beans, mustard, vinegar, a little reserved water, salt, and pepper; mash to desired consistency.
Heat oil in a wok/skillet. Sauté garlic (and onion if using) 30–60 sec. Add mushrooms; cook until lightly browned. Add kale; stir-fry until wilted. Season.
Mix ketjap manis and agave syrup; add cashews (or keep for topping).
Fold stir-fried vegetables into the mash. Adjust mustard, vinegar, salt, and pepper to taste.
Serve with the ketjap–agave–cashew dressing.
recipe_tips: Use any plant-based milk if needed to adjust mash consistency
Ketjap manis is Indonesian sweet soy sauce; substitute with regular soy sauce + a bit more agave if unavailable

name: Lactose-free high-protein spinach artichoke dip
ingredients: 80g frozen spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F)
Finely chop the artichoke hearts and mince the garlic
Blend lactose-free cottage cheese and Greek yogurt in food processor until smooth and creamy
In an oven-safe dish, mix blended cheese mixture with chopped artichokes, squeezed spinach, garlic, salt, and pepper
Top with lactose-free mozzarella
Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden and bubbly
Let cool for 5 minutes before serving
Serve warm with tortilla chips, crackers, or vegetable sticks
recipe_benefits: High-protein snack perfect for muscle recovery
Iron-rich spinach supports blood health during menstrual phase
Artichokes provide prebiotic fiber for gut health
Lactose-free and easy to digest
Warm and comforting, great for hormonal cravings
recipe_tips: Squeeze spinach VERY well to remove excess water - this prevents a watery dip
Blending the cottage cheese makes it ultra-smooth like the original recipe
Use brands like Arla Lactofree, Valio, or Green Valley for lactose-free dairy
Can be made ahead and refrigerated, reheat at 180°C for 10 minutes
Fresh garlic cloves give much better flavor than garlic powder
Serve with colorful bell pepper strips for extra nutrition
recipe_notes: Inspired by a viral Instagram recipe from @thekelseyrose, this lactose-free version uses lactose-free cottage cheese and Greek yogurt for an authentic taste that's virtually identical to the original. Perfect for those avoiding lactose while still enjoying that creamy, protein-packed dip!

name: Vegan pecan caramel tart
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (fan/convection)
In a food processor, grind the oats and almond flour until fine
Add the plant-based butter, maple syrup, and salt to processor; process again to combine
Press the mixture into a 20cm tart pan with removable bottom - firm up the sides first to make a rim about 0.5cm thick, then press the base evenly
Bake the crust for 15 minutes until golden, then let cool for 30 minutes
Toast the pecans in a skillet until golden to enhance flavor; set aside
Make the caramel: In a saucepan, warm the coconut oil, plant-based butter, and maple syrup over moderate heat, stirring until melted
Add the almond butter and cinnamon to caramel and stir until fully combined
Let the caramel cool for 15 minutes to thicken, then pour over the cooled crust
Refrigerate the tart for at least 4 hours to set
Before serving, top with the toasted pecans and slice into 8 pieces

benefits: Rich in healthy fats from nuts and seeds

recipe_benefits: Completely plant-based and vegan-friendly
Gluten-free with almond flour and oat base
Rich in healthy fats from nuts and coconut oil
Natural sweetener (maple syrup) instead of refined sugar
Perfect make-ahead dessert for entertaining
recipe_tips: Store the tart in the refrigerator for best texture - caramel softens at room temperature
Toast pecans just before serving for maximum crunch and flavor
Let caramel cool for 15 minutes before pouring to prevent it from soaking into the crust
Use a tart pan with removable bottom for easy serving
Can be made 1-2 days ahead and kept refrigerated
recipe_notes: This indulgent vegan caramel tart features a nutty oat crust, rich maple-cinnamon caramel layer, and toasted pecans. Requires 4 hours setting time in refrigerator. Serves 8 generous portions.

name: Ayurvedic pumpkin curry with spinach
instructions: Finely mince the ginger and garlic. Dice the onion and bell pepper.
Add the butternut squash cubes to a pot of boiling water and cook for 10 minutes until tender. Drain.
Melt the butter in a pan. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger; sauté for a few minutes until golden.
Stir in the garam masala, cumin, and cayenne; cook for 1 minute.
Add the diced bell pepper and cook for a few minutes.
Add the drained lentils and cooked butternut squash. Pour in the tomato passata and coconut milk, season with salt, and simmer over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add the fresh spinach and stir until wilted (about 2 minutes).
Serve with flatbread, naan, or rice, and garnish with fresh cilantro.

name: Salted tahini hot chocolate
instructions: Lightly crush the cardamom pod in a mortar, remove the green shell and grind the seeds finely.
Put all ingredients (except the rose petals) in a small saucepan and bring to a boil.
Stir everything well and keep cooking until the hot chocolate starts to bubble and thickens.
Pour into a cup and garnish with extra cinnamon and, optionally, rose petals.
recipe_benefits: Rich in magnesium from tahini and cacao
Antioxidants from cacao support cellular health
Anti-inflammatory spices (cinnamon, cardamom, clove)
Healthy fats from tahini
recipe_notes: Optional: garnish with dried rose petals for extra flavor

name: Hormone balancing butternut squash mac and cheese
instructions: If using cashews, soak in water for 2-4 hours until soft. Drain and rinse before use.
Preheat oven to 220°C and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Spread butternut squash on sheet, drizzle with avocado oil, salt and pepper. Toss to coat.
Roast for 30-40 minutes, flipping halfway through until fork tender. Let cool for 5 minutes.

Add all sauce ingredients (roasted squash, plant-based milk, cashews, nutritional yeast, garlic, lemon juice, onion powder, paprika powder, chili powder, salt, liquid smoke, hot sauce) to a blender and blend until smooth.
Cook pasta according to package directions.
Add drained pasta back to pot. Pour on the sauce, add chopped spinach and stir to combine.
Cook over medium-low heat until heated throughout and serve immediately.

Vitamin C from squash boosts progesterone levels
B vitamins from nutritional yeast support hormone health
Fiber-rich to support luteal phase energy

name: Luteal anti-inflammatory stuffed sweet potato
instructions: Pierce sweet potato a few times with a fork. Air fry at 200°C for 30-40 minutes until completely soft, flipping halfway through. (Alternative: Bake in oven at 200°C for 1-2 hours). Slice open down the middle and set aside to cool.

Make the creamy peanut sauce by adding peanut butter, coconut aminos, water, rice vinegar, maple syrup, minced ginger, minced garlic, and sriracha to a blender. Mix until creamy and well combined. Set aside.

Heat a pan over medium heat and add oil, then stir in chickpeas with garlic powder, cumin powder, ginger powder, coriander, maple syrup, and coconut aminos until chickpeas are fragrant and crispy (about 5 minutes).
Add kale and minced garlic to the pan and cook another couple of minutes until greens are wilted.
Assemble the sweet potato by adding the greens and chickpea mixture, then drizzle with creamy peanut sauce.
Top with cilantro, green onion, chopped peanuts and more sriracha if desired.
recipe_benefits: Complex carbs from sweet potato for sustained energy
Anti-inflammatory spices (ginger, cumin, coriander)
Protein and fiber from chickpeas support hormone balance
Vitamin A from sweet potato supports skin health
Magnesium from kale and chickpeas for luteal phase support
Healthy fats from peanut butter
recipe_tips: Bake sweet potato the night before to save time
Extra peanut sauce can be used for other bowls throughout the week
Stores well in fridge for 3-4 days
recipe_notes: Anti-inflammatory luteal phase recipe with sustained energy and hormone balancing nutrients

name: Pumpkin Cheesecake with Candied Apples
instructions: Soak cashews in hot water for at least 2 hours or overnight.

Brown the butter: Place butter in a small saucepan over medium-low heat and let it melt, swirling the pan as it cooks. Continue until the milk solids turn golden, smell nutty, and become fragrant (about 3 minutes). Watch carefully so it doesn't burn.
Add graham crackers to a food processor and process until the texture is like sand. Transfer to a bowl and stir in the brown butter, mixing until thoroughly coated like wet sand.
Press the crust firmly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan (no parchment needed). Rinse food processor.

Drain the soaked cashews and add to the food processor along with pumpkin puree, 160ml maple syrup, coconut oil, coconut milk, lemon juice, vanilla extract, pumpkin pie spice, and salt. Process until completely smooth (about 5 minutes).
Make the candied apples: Add diced apples to a small pan over medium heat with water, 30ml maple syrup, and cinnamon. Cook about 5 minutes until softened.
Pour half the pumpkin mixture into the springform pan, then add all of the apples. Pour the rest of the pumpkin mixture on top, using a spatula to ensure an even layer.
Freeze for at least 4 hours or until firm. You can freeze overnight or for up to 1 week.
Thaw on the countertop at room temperature for about 30 minutes before serving.

benefits: Rich in vitamin A from pumpkin

recipe_benefits: Pumpkin is rich in beta-carotene and vitamin A for skin health
Cashews provide healthy fats and minerals
Apples add fiber and natural sweetness
recipe_tips: Soak cashews overnight for best results
Watch the butter carefully when browning to prevent burning
Make sure the pumpkin mixture is completely smooth for best texture
Can be frozen for up to 1 week
recipe_notes: Makes 12 servings. This vegan pumpkin cheesecake has a graham cracker crust, creamy pumpkin filling, and candied apple layer. Perfect for fall gatherings.

Place the egg-avocado scramble on the tortilla.
Top with fresh spinach leaves, halved cherry tomatoes, and crumbled aged cheese.
Make the dip: Mix Greek yogurt with lemon juice, finely chopped dill, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Fold the tortilla into a wrap and serve with the herb yogurt dip on the side.

name: Marry Me Mushroom Pasta
instructions: Make cashew cream: Soak cashews in hot water for 30 minutes, then drain and blend with water until smooth and creamy
Slice king oyster mushroom stems lengthwise into thick slabs (about 1cm thick). Score the surface with a crosshatch pattern using a knife - this helps them absorb flavors
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Place mushroom slabs in the pan and DO NOT MOVE them for 3-4 minutes - this creates a golden, caramelized crust (the viral technique for meaty texture)
Flip mushrooms and sear the other side until golden brown. Remove from pan
In the same pan, sauté garlic until fragrant
Add sun-dried tomatoes and vegetable stock, let simmer briefly
Pour in the cashew cream and add nutritional yeast, stir to create a creamy sauce
Return mushrooms to the pan, simmer gently until everything is heated through
Serve over cooked pasta and garnish with fresh basil

benefits: Social media sensation adapted for vegetarians
Named for being so good it'll make someone propose

recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. This plant-based version maintains all the viral appeal while being completely vegetarian. For gluten-free diets, simply substitute with gluten-free pasta (rice, corn, or chickpea pasta work perfectly).

instructions: Make arepa dough: mix masarepa, warm water, salt, and olive oil until smooth. Let rest 5 minutes

Heat a lightly oiled griddle or pan over medium heat
Cook arepas 5-7 minutes per side until golden crust forms and sound hollow when tapped
While arepas cook, warm black beans with cumin, salt and pepper
Prepare filling: mix diced tomato, sliced red onion, cilantro, and lime juice

Layer with warm black beans, crumbled vegan feta, avocado slices, and tomato-onion mixture
Serve immediately

name: Creamy mushroom pasta
ingredients: ½ tbsp extra virgin olive oil
¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Brush mushrooms clean and cut into large chunks
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat
Add sliced onion and cook until translucent (3-4 minutes)
Add mushrooms and cook for 5-7 minutes until golden brown
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute
Add garlic granules, chili flakes, dried parsley, and oregano - stir well
Pour in white wine or water and simmer for 5 minutes until slightly reduced
Stir in dairy cream and simmer gently for 5-10 minutes until sauce thickens
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Toss cooked pasta with the creamy mushroom sauce
Top with chopped fresh parsley and serve

benefits: Rich in B vitamins from mushrooms

recipe_tips: Use a mix of mushroom varieties for deeper flavor (portobello, chestnut, shiitake)
Don't wash mushrooms - brush them clean to prevent sogginess
Let mushrooms brown without stirring too much for best flavor
Pasta geveltjes (gable-shaped pasta) works perfectly for catching the sauce
Add a splash of pasta water if sauce becomes too thick
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Rich and creamy mushroom pasta with white wine, garlic, and chili flakes. Classic Italian comfort food ready in 25 minutes.

Add diced carrot and zucchini, cook for another 3 minutes
Stir in turmeric, cumin, salt, and black pepper

name: Rustic Tomato Pasta Bake with Herbed Breadcrumbs
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C fan / 390°F, grill setting
Make breadcrumbs by pulsing bread in blender until coarse crumbs form
Heat 30ml olive oil in large pan over medium heat
Add diced onion, garlic, and sun-dried tomatoes with pinch of salt
Cook 5-10 minutes until onions soften, stirring through oregano
Add tomato paste, canned tomatoes, and balsamic vinegar
Mix well and simmer 10-15 minutes until sauce thickens
Meanwhile, cook pasta according to package instructions and drain
For white sauce: heat remaining oil and flour in medium pan
Whisk to form smooth paste, cook 2-3 minutes over medium-high heat
Gradually stream in oat milk, whisking constantly until smooth and thick
Add nutritional yeast, mustard, crumbled tofu, salt and pepper
Combine pasta with tomato sauce in baking dish
Spoon white sauce over top and scatter with breadcrumbs
Bake 5-10 minutes until golden and bubbling
recipe_tips: Use good-quality sourdough for best breadcrumb texture
Don't let the flour burn when making the white sauce
Crumble tofu finely for better integration
recipe_notes: A comforting pasta bake with rich tomato sauce and creamy protein-packed white sauce

name: Rustic Tomato Pasta Bake with Herbed Breadcrumbs
ingredients: 20ml rice flour + 7.5ml tapioca starch + 2.5ml xanthan gum
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C fan / 390°F, grill setting
Make breadcrumbs by pulsing gluten-free bread in blender until coarse crumbs form
Heat 30ml olive oil in large pan over medium heat
Add diced onion, garlic, and sun-dried tomatoes with pinch of salt
Cook 5-10 minutes until onions soften, stirring through oregano
Add tomato paste, canned tomatoes, and balsamic vinegar
Mix well and simmer 10-15 minutes until sauce thickens
Meanwhile, cook gluten-free pasta according to package instructions and drain
For white sauce: heat remaining oil and rice flour mixture in medium pan
Whisk to form smooth paste, cook 2-3 minutes over medium-high heat (rice flour creates better texture)
Gradually stream in oat milk, whisking constantly until smooth and thick
Add nutritional yeast, mustard, cooked lentils, hemp hearts, salt and pepper
Combine pasta with tomato sauce in baking dish
Spoon white sauce over top and scatter with breadcrumbs
Bake 5-10 minutes until golden and bubbling
recipe_tips: Use certified gluten-free bread for safe breadcrumbs
Rice flour + tapioca starch + xanthan gum creates better texture than single flour
Pre-cooked lentils work best - canned or leftover home-cooked
Hemp hearts add extra protein and nutty flavor
recipe_notes: Gluten-free version using rice flour blend with xanthan gum for optimal texture, plus lentils and hemp hearts for extra plant protein

name: Protein-Packed Quinoa Breakfast Porridge with Banana & Peanut Butter
instructions: Place cooked quinoa, porridge oats, oat milk, and frozen blueberries in small saucepan
Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently until oat milk is absorbed and oats are tender (4-5 minutes)
Alternatively, microwave on high for 1 minute at a time, stirring between intervals until tender
Slice half the banana into the pan and gently mash with fork while stirring through porridge
Transfer to serving bowl and top with remaining banana slices
Add dollop of peanut butter and sprinkle with ground flaxseeds
Dust with cinnamon and serve immediately while warm
recipe_tips: Use pre-cooked quinoa to save time - cook a batch ahead
Frozen blueberries work better than fresh as they break down slightly
Adjust liquid if porridge becomes too thick
recipe_notes: A protein-rich breakfast combining quinoa's complete amino acids with oats for sustained energy

name: Chickpea, Tofu & Harissa Stew
instructions: Heat sesame oil in large frying pan over medium heat
Add diced onion with pinch of salt, cook stirring often until softened (about 10 minutes)
Reduce heat to low if necessary to prevent browning
Add minced garlic and cook, stirring frequently, for 2 minutes
Stir in cumin and paprika, cook stirring throughout until fragrant (about 1 minute)
Add chickpeas, cubed tofu, chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, and harissa paste
Cook stirring often until tomatoes have darkened and reduced (10-15 minutes)
Stir in fresh spinach and allow to wilt (1-2 minutes)
Add tamari, maple syrup, and lemon juice
Taste and season with salt and pepper as needed
Remove from heat and stir in coconut yogurt before serving
recipe_tips: Harissa spice levels vary by brand - taste yours first and adjust amount accordingly
If stew becomes too spicy, add more coconut yogurt to mellow it out
Serve with roasted sweet potatoes, rice noodles, or brown rice as suggested
recipe_notes: A hearty plant-based stew packed with protein from chickpeas and tofu, with warming North African spices

name: Healthy Chicken Kapsalon
instructions: Cut sweet potatoes into thick fries. Toss with olive oil, paprika powder, salt and pepper.
Bake fries at 220°C for 25-30 minutes until crispy, flipping halfway through.
Season chicken breast strips with cumin, garlic powder, oregano, salt and pepper. Cook in a pan until golden and cooked through.
Make tzatziki sauce: Scrape out the seeds from 1/4 cucumber and finely grate. Squeeze out excess liquid. Mix with coconut yogurt, minced garlic, and lemon juice. Season with salt.
Shred iceberg lettuce and thinly slice red cabbage. Dice tomato and remaining cucumber. Slice red onion.
Layer fries in a bowl, top with lettuce, red cabbage, tomato, cucumber, and red onion. Add cooked chicken.
Drizzle with homemade tzatziki sauce and sprinkle with pumpkin seeds.
recipe_tips: Soak sweet potato fries in cold water for 30 minutes before roasting for extra crispiness
Don't overcrowd the baking tray - use two trays if needed for better roasting
Adjust sriracha amount in sauce according to spice preference
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Healthy take on Dutch kapsalon using sweet potato fries, lean seasoned chicken, fresh salad, and spicy yogurt sauce

name: Thai Red Lentil & Chickpea Curry
ingredients: 2 whole bird's eye chillies, finely chopped
4 whole large sweet pointed red peppers, cut into 1cm slices
instructions: Warm olive oil in large saucepan set over medium-low heat
Add garlic, ginger, chillies and curry paste
Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant
Add red peppers, red lentils, coconut milk, tamari, maple syrup and vegetable stock
Bring to boil then reduce heat and simmer with lid on for 20-25 minutes until lentils are tender
Add chickpeas along with liquid from jar and simmer for 5-10 minutes
Add spinach and cook until just wilted
Stir in lime juice and three-quarters of Thai basil
Taste and adjust seasoning
Serve in bowls, topped with remaining Thai basil, extra chillies, and lime wedges
Delicious with steamed sticky rice or quinoa
recipe_tips: Keep curry mild for sensitive palates or add extra chillies for heat lovers
Curry keeps well in fridge for up to 3 days or freezer for up to 3 months
Let cool completely before storing in airtight containers for best results
recipe_notes: One-pan curry perfect for batch-cooking - rich, fragrant, and packed with lentils, chickpeas and spinach for fiber, protein and iron

name: Crispy Herb-Crusted Chicken with Spiced Tomato Sauce
instructions: Cut chicken breast into bite-sized chunks, season with salt and pepper
Make spiced tomato sauce: heat olive oil, sauté chopped shallots until soft
Add minced garlic, cook 1 minute, then add crushed tomatoes, sugar, salt, pepper
Simmer sauce 15 minutes until thickened, stir in fresh basil at end
Set up breading station: seasoned flour, milk, panko with paprika and oregano
Dredge chicken chunks: flour, then milk, then seasoned panko, pressing gently
Heat olive oil in large pan over medium-high heat
Fry breaded chicken pieces 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through
Check internal temperature reaches 75°C
Season with extra paprika and oregano while hot
Serve immediately over warm spiced tomato sauce with fresh basil
recipe_tips: Cut chicken evenly for consistent cooking
Don't overcrowd pan - fry in batches if needed
Press panko coating gently to help it stick
Fresh basil added at end preserves bright flavor

recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Crispy herb-crusted chicken with vibrant tomato sauce creates a satisfying comfort food that delivers excellent protein while maintaining indulgent flavors.

name: Mediterranean Lactose-Free Feta & Chickpea Cauliflower Rice Bowl
instructions: If using fresh cauliflower, pulse florets in food processor until rice-sized pieces form
Heat 15ml olive oil in large frying pan over medium heat
Add cauliflower rice and minced garlic, cook for 5-7 minutes until tender
Season cauliflower rice with oregano, salt, pepper, and fresh herbs
Meanwhile, prepare dressing by whisking tahini, lemon juice, remaining olive oil, salt and pepper
Toast pine nuts in dry pan for 2-3 minutes until lightly golden
In large serving bowl, combine warm cauliflower rice with chickpeas
Add bell pepper, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and cucumber
Drizzle with tahini lemon dressing and toss gently to combine
Top with crumbled lactose-free feta cheese, toasted pine nuts, and hemp hearts
Garnish with fresh herbs before serving
recipe_tips: Violife or other quality dairy-free feta provides authentic Mediterranean flavor
Let lactose-free feta come to room temperature before crumbling for best texture
Perfect for those with lactose intolerance who love Mediterranean flavors

Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures

name: Earl Grey Tea-Infused Quinoa Porridge with Caramelized Persimmon
instructions: Prepare Earl Grey tea: Brew 2 strong tea bags in 200ml boiling water for 5 minutes, then remove bags and cool completely
Cook quinoa ahead with tea: Rinse 200g quinoa and simmer in 300ml Earl Grey tea + 100ml water for 15 minutes until tender. Store in fridge for up to 5 days
In medium saucepan, combine oats, 80g tea-infused pre-cooked quinoa, oat milk, remaining Earl Grey tea, and pinch of salt
Bring to gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally
Meanwhile, heat small frying pan over medium heat
Add diced persimmon with half the maple syrup and cinnamon
Cook persimmon gently for 6-8 minutes until caramelized and jammy
When porridge is creamy and Earl Grey-scented, remove from heat
Stir in protein powder, vanilla extract, ginger, and cardamom until smooth
Add hemp hearts and remaining maple syrup, mix well
Serve in warm bowl topped with caramelized persimmon
Sprinkle with chopped walnuts, pumpkin seeds, and extra cinnamon
recipe_tips: Earl Grey's bergamot adds elegant citrus notes that pair beautifully with persimmon
If persimmons aren't available, use very ripe pears for similar sweetness
Tea-infused quinoa can be batch-cooked and adds sophisticated flavor to the porridge
recipe_notes: Elegant breakfast porridge infused with Earl Grey tea's bergamot essence, topped with caramelized persimmon for natural sweetness and winter warmth

name: Chocolate Overnight Oats with Dark Chocolate Crumble
instructions: In a jar or container, mix together oats, cocoa protein powder, cocoa powder, flax seeds, and chia seeds
Add soy milk, sweetener, and vanilla extract, stir well to combine all dry ingredients
Pour the mixture into your storage jar and refrigerate overnight or at least 1 hour
In separate small bowl, mix vanilla yogurt - this will be your middle layer
Melt dark chocolate with coconut oil in microwave or double boiler until smooth
Crush rice cake into small pieces and mix with half the melted chocolate
When ready to serve, layer the overnight oats base in serving jar
Add yogurt layer on top of the oat base
Top with chocolate rice cake crumble and drizzle remaining melted chocolate
Refrigerate for 1 hour or overnight before enjoying for best texture

benefits: high fiber from oats and seeds
antioxidants from cocoa and dark chocolate

recipe_tips: Prepare multiple jars at once for easy grab-and-go breakfasts
Cocoa protein powder provides rich chocolate flavor while boosting protein content
Rice cake adds satisfying crunch - can substitute with granola if preferred
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Indulgent chocolate overnight oats with impressive protein content, perfect for meal prep and satisfying chocolate cravings healthily

name: Caramelized Fig & Pistachio Breakfast Bowl
instructions: Begin by slicing the figs in half (or quarters if you prefer larger pieces)
Heat a medium frying pan over medium-low heat
Melt the butter and cinnamon in the pan, stirring to combine
Drizzle the honey into the butter mixture and stir with a wooden spoon
Gently place the fig halves cut-side down in the pan
Leave for 3-4 minutes to slowly caramelize without stirring
Once the figs are nicely soft and jammy, remove from pan and set aside on a plate
Using the same pan with the remaining juices, add the oats and pistachios
Stir continuously and cook for 2-3 minutes until the oats have toasted and browned nicely
Place the Greek yogurt in a serving bowl
Top the yogurt with the toasted oat and pistachio mixture
Arrange the caramelized figs on top of the granola mix
Drizzle with extra honey if desired and serve immediately
recipe_benefits: High fiber content supports digestive health
Natural sugars from figs provide sustained energy
Pistachios offer healthy fats and protein
Cinnamon helps regulate blood sugar
Probiotics from yogurt support gut health
Seasonal autumn fruit provides antioxidants
recipe_tips: Choose figs that are ripe but still firm - they'll caramelize better without falling apart
Don't move the figs while caramelizing to get a beautiful golden color
Save the pan juices to toast the oats - they add incredible flavor
This recipe works beautifully with any type of yogurt you prefer
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. A stunning autumn breakfast that celebrates fresh figs at their peak, with warm spices and crunchy pistachios creating the perfect seasonal start to your day

name: Power Oats
instructions: In a small saucepan, bring water to a boil with a pinch of salt
Add rolled oats and reduce heat to medium-low
Simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally until creamy
Stir in chia seeds, hemp hearts, and ground flax seeds
Cook for another 2 minutes until seeds are well incorporated

Transfer to serving bowl
Top with almond butter, sliced banana, and mixed berries
Sprinkle with pumpkin seeds and sunflower seeds
Serve hot

name: Superfood Breakfast Bowl
instructions: Add frozen bananas, açai puree, and mixed frozen berries to a high-speed blender
Add coconut milk and 1 tbsp almond butter
Blend until thick and smooth, like soft-serve ice cream consistency
You may need to stop and scrape down sides several times
The mixture should be thick enough to eat with a spoon
Transfer to a chilled bowl
Top with granola, sliced kiwi, and goji berries
Drizzle with remaining almond butter
Sprinkle with coconut flakes, chia seeds, and mint leaves
Serve immediately

High vitamin C content supports immune system
Natural sugars from fruits provide quick energy
Fiber and healthy fats for sustained satiety
Potassium supports heart health and muscle function
recipe_tips: Use frozen fruit for the best thick, creamy texture
Don't add too much liquid - you want a thick smoothie base
Arrange toppings in rows for an Instagram-worthy presentation
Eat immediately as the base will start to melt
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Vibrant superfood bowl packed with antioxidants and natural energy - like a healthy ice cream sundae for breakfast

name: Strawberry Protein Smoothie
instructions: Add cashew milk, frozen banana, and frozen strawberries to blender
Add rolled oats, chia seeds, and hemp hearts
Pour in fresh lime juice and vanilla extract
Add protein powder and almond butter
Add maple syrup if extra sweetness is desired
Blend on high speed for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth
Stop and scrape down sides if needed
Blend again until creamy and no chunks remain
Pour into large glass and serve immediately
recipe_benefits: High protein from protein powder and hemp hearts
Vitamin C from strawberries supports immune function
Omega-3 fatty acids from chia seeds and hemp hearts
Complex carbohydrates from oats provide sustained energy
Potassium from banana supports muscle function

Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures

instructions: Add coconut milk and Greek yogurt to blender first
Add fresh spinach leaves, packing them down

The color should be a beautiful pale green
Pour into glass and serve immediately

Spinach adds iron and folate without affecting taste
High protein supports muscle recovery and satiety
Antioxidants from matcha and spinach fight inflammation
Probiotics from yogurt support digestive health

Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Elegant green smoothie with subtle matcha flavor and hidden vegetables - like drinking a nutritious cloud

name: Very Berry Smoothie
instructions: Add almond milk to blender first
Add frozen banana, strawberries, and blueberries
Add almond butter, chia seeds, and hemp hearts
Pour in vanilla extract and maple syrup
Add ice cubes for extra thickness
Blend on high speed for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth

name: Super Simple Quinoa Chilli
instructions: Place a large, heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat and add a drizzle of olive oil
Once hot, add the red onion, garlic and a sprinkle of salt and pepper
Cook for 4-5 minutes, until softened and translucent
Add the paprika, cumin and tomato purée, stirring until everything is coated in the spices and tomato

Add in the quinoa, drained chickpeas and kidney beans, and the tin of tomatoes
Fill the empty tin with water and add this liquid to the pan
Cover the pan and leave to simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent the quinoa from sticking
Once the quinoa is tender, stir in the almond butter, lime juice, miso and maple syrup
Adjust seasoning to taste, and serve with extra lime squeezed over

Plant-based milks may have different consistencies

name: Pre-Workout Protein Smoothie

recipe_tips: Use frozen berries for a thicker smoothie texture

instructions: Drain and rinse soaked cashews thoroughly
Add cashews, protein powder, cocoa powder to high-speed blender

instructions: Sift matcha powder into a small bowl to remove lumps
Add 60ml hot (not boiling) plant milk to matcha

name: Breakfast Muffins
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C fan/375°F
Grease 6-cup muffin tin or line with cases
Beat eggs in large bowl, whisk in milk and olive oil
Add flour, salt and pepper, mix until just combined

name: Chicken Crust Casserole

Brown ground chicken in olive oil until cooked through
Beat eggs with cream, half the cheddar and seasonings
Layer chicken and vegetables in greased baking dish
Pour egg mixture over, top with remaining cheese
Sprinkle with parmesan and fresh herbs
Bake 25-30 minutes until set and golden

benefits: complete protein from chicken and eggs

Plant-based milks may have different consistencies

Heat coconut oil in non-stick pan over medium heat
Pour 1/3 cup batter per pancake

recipe_tips: Don't flip too early or pancakes will break
Keep cooked pancakes warm in low oven

Plant-based milks may have different consistencies

name: Protein Truffle Bites
instructions: Mix protein powder, oats, cocoa powder, chia seeds and salt in bowl
In separate bowl, mix almond butter, maple syrup and melted coconut oil
Combine wet and dry ingredients until mixture holds together
Add water 1 tsp at a time if too dry
Chill mixture 15 minutes until firm
Roll into 8 small balls with damp hands
Roll each ball in cocoa powder to coat
Store in fridge until ready to eat
recipe_tips: Chill mixture if too soft to roll
Use damp hands to prevent sticking
Store in airtight container for up to 1 week
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies

instructions: Line 6 mini muffin cases in tin
Melt 40g chocolate with 1 tbsp coconut oil gently
Spoon thin layer into each case, chill 10 minutes
Mix peanut butter with maple syrup and salt

recipe_tips: Don't overheat chocolate or it will seize

recipe_notes: Natural chocolate treats providing magnesium for muscle relaxation and mood support during menstruation

instructions: Soak dates in warm water for 10 minutes if very dry
Add dates to food processor, pulse until paste forms
Add oats, pulse until roughly chopped
Add almond butter, chia seeds, hemp hearts, cinnamon and salt

Arrange smoked salmon pieces on top
Garnish with capers and extra dill

recipe_tips: Choose firm cucumber for best bite
Pat salmon dry before arranging
Make just before serving to prevent soggy cucumber
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies

Divide the lentil-pesto mixture evenly over the mushroom caps
Place stuffed mushrooms in an oven dish
Bake for 20 minutes at 200°C until mushrooms are tender
Serve immediately while hot
recipe_benefits: Portobello mushrooms provide meaty texture and B vitamins

Serve with extra pesto drizzled on top for maximum flavor
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Protein-packed version of classic stuffed mushrooms with lentils and silken tofu pesto. This creates a complete, satisfying meal with exceptional nutritional value.

recipe_benefits: Avocado provides healthy monounsaturated fats and fiber

instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Peel and cube the sweet potato into 2cm pieces
Spread sweet potato cubes on a baking tray lined with baking paper
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper
Roast in preheated oven for about 25 minutes until golden and tender
Meanwhile, cut the red bell pepper and cucumber into cubes
Heat falafel briefly in a pan or oven according to package instructions
Arrange roasted sweet potatoes in serving bowl
Add warmed falafel, red bell pepper cubes, cucumber, and crumbled lactose-free feta

Season with salt and pepper to taste

name: Plant-Based Chocolate Mousse with Rice Cakes
instructions: Prepare espresso or dissolve 1 tsp instant coffee in 50ml hot water
Soak both rice cakes in the espresso until softened but not falling apart

name: Vegetarian High-Protein Power Bowl with Tofu & Sweet Potato

Breng de tofublokjes op smaak met knoflookpoeder, kurkuma, zwarte peper en resterende zout

Bak de tofublokjes 8-10 minuten en draai regelmatig om tot ze rondom goudbruin zijn
Snijd de avocado en schik in een serveerbowl

Verdeel de geroosterde zoete aardappel en goudbruine tofu in aparte secties

name: High-protein pistachio cheesecake
instructions: Preheat oven to 163°C (325°F)
Add all ingredients (cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, egg, pistachios, maple syrup, almond flour, and vanilla) to a blender
Blend on high speed until completely smooth with no pistachio chunks remaining
Pour the mixture into a small ramekin or individual baking dish (about 10cm/4 inches) lined with parchment paper
Bake for 15-20 minutes. The edges should feel set but the center should still have a slight jiggle when you move the dish
Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature
Transfer to the fridge and chill for at least 2-4 hours (overnight is best)
Serve chilled, optionally topped with extra crushed pistachios
