Check internal temperature reaches 75°C
Season with extra paprika and oregano while hot
Serve immediately over warm spiced tomato sauce with fresh basil
recipe_tips: Cut chicken evenly for consistent cooking
Don't overcrowd pan - fry in batches if needed
Press panko coating gently to help it stick
Fresh basil added at end preserves bright flavor
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Crispy herb-crusted chicken with vibrant tomato sauce creates a satisfying comfort food that delivers excellent protein while maintaining indulgent flavors.

name: Mediterranean plant-based Cauliflower Rice Bowl
instructions: If using fresh cauliflower, pulse florets in food processor until rice-sized pieces form
Season diced chicken with oregano, salt, pepper, and half the minced garlic
Heat 15ml olive oil in large frying pan over medium-high heat
Cook seasoned chicken for 6-8 minutes until golden brown and cooked through, set aside
In same pan, add cauliflower rice and remaining garlic, cook for 5-7 minutes until tender
Season cauliflower rice with salt, pepper, and fresh herbs
Meanwhile, prepare dressing by whisking tahini, lemon juice, remaining olive oil, salt and pepper
In large serving bowl, combine warm cauliflower rice and cooked chicken
Add chickpeas, bell pepper, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and cucumber
Drizzle with tahini lemon dressing and toss gently to combine
Sprinkle with hemp hearts and fresh herbs before serving

name: Hearty plant-based meat & Vegetable Stew
ingredients: 150g lean Beyond Meat gehakt stewing meat, cubed
instructions: Heat olive oil in heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat
Season beef cubes with salt and pepper
Brown beef on all sides (5-6 minutes total)
Add diced onion and cook until softened (5 minutes)
Add garlic and cook 1 minute until fragrant
Add chopped carrots, celery, and potato
Pour in beef stock and canned tomatoes
Add thyme and bay leaves
Bring to boil, then reduce heat to low
Cover and simmer 60-75 minutes until beef is tender
Remove bay leaves before serving
Adjust seasoning to taste
recipe_tips: Brown the beef well for deeper flavor
Don't rush the simmering - tender beef takes time
Make extra for leftovers - tastes even better next day
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Rich in heme iron for optimal absorption, perfect for replenishing iron during menstruation

name: BEA Salad (plant-based bacon, Egg, Avocado)
instructions: Bring water to boil in small saucepan
Gently lower eggs into boiling water
Cook for 6-7 minutes for soft-boiled, 8-9 for medium
Transfer eggs to cold water to stop cooking
Cook bacon in dry pan until crispy (4-5 minutes)
Arrange mixed greens in serving bowl
Add sliced cucumber and halved cherry tomatoes
Peel and slice avocado, arrange on top
Carefully peel soft-boiled eggs and halve
Place egg halves on salad
Sprinkle with crispy bacon pieces
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice, season to taste
recipe_tips: Use very fresh eggs for perfect soft-boiled texture
Don't overdress - let the ingredients shine
Serve immediately while eggs are warm
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Protein and healthy fat powerhouse with choline for hormone support during menstruation

name: Coconut Meatballs
instructions: Mix ground beef, coconut, egg, minced garlic and herbs in bowl
Add cumin, coriander, salt and pepper
Mix gently with hands until just combined
Roll into 10 equal meatballs
Heat coconut oil in large pan over medium heat
Cook meatballs 12-15 minutes, turning every 3-4 minutes
Cook until golden brown and cooked through
Serve with steamed vegetables or rice
recipe_tips: Don't overmix meat or meatballs will be tough
Make sure oil is hot before adding meatballs
Test one meatball first to check seasoning
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Iron-rich meatballs with healthy coconut fats for long-lasting energy and reduced cravings during menstruation

name: plant-based protein Crust Casserole
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C fan/355°F
Brown ground chicken in olive oil until cooked through
Beat eggs with cream, half the cheddar and seasonings
Layer chicken and vegetables in greased baking dish
Pour egg mixture over, top with remaining cheese
Sprinkle with parmesan and fresh herbs
Bake 25-30 minutes until set and golden
Rest 5 minutes before serving

benefits: complete protein from chicken and eggs

recipe_tips: Drain excess liquid from vegetables before layering
Don't overbake or eggs will become rubbery
Can be made ahead and reheated
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. High protein casserole supporting muscle recovery and stable blood sugar, with calcium for hormone health during menstruation

name: Egg & plant-based meat Protein Bowl
instructions: Heat olive oil in large pan over medium-high heat
Add ground beef, cook 5-6 minutes breaking up with spoon
Add minced garlic, paprika, cumin, salt and pepper
Cook 2 minutes until beef is browned and cooked through
Push beef to one side, add spinach to pan
Cook until wilted, about 2 minutes
Crack eggs into pan, scramble with beef and spinach
Serve in bowl topped with sliced ripe avocado
recipe_tips: Don't overcook eggs - they continue cooking in hot pan
Use grass-fed beef for better nutrition
Add ripe avocado just before serving
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Powerhouse of heme iron and complete protein to restore energy and support hormone health during menstruation

name: Garlic Butter plant-based protein
instructions: Season chicken breast with salt and pepper
Heat 1 tbsp butter in pan over medium-high heat
Cook chicken 6-7 minutes per side until golden and cooked through
Remove chicken, rest under foil
Add remaining butter and minced garlic to pan
Cook 1 minute until fragrant
Add wine, thyme, rosemary and lemon juice
Simmer 2 minutes, pour over sliced chicken
recipe_tips: Don't move chicken too early or it won't brown
Use meat thermometer - internal temp should reach 75°C
Rest chicken 5 minutes before slicing
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. High-protein chicken supporting energy levels and hormone balance with zinc and B vitamins during menstruation

name: Burgers with Sweet Potato Fries and Grilled Vegetables
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Cut sweet potatoes into wedges, toss with olive oil, salt, and pepper
Roast sweet potato wedges for about 25 minutes until golden and tender
If using fresh mince: mix beef with garlic, beaten egg, and breadcrumbs, then shape into patty
If using pre-made burger: season lightly with garlic and cook directly
Grill or pan-fry the burger patty until cooked through (internal temp 70°C)
Meanwhile, grill eggplant and zucchini slices until tender and slightly charred
Toast or warm the burger bun
Assemble burger with patty, grilled vegetables, bean sprouts, and fresh salad
Serve with roasted sweet potato fries on the side

benefits: Balanced nutrition with protein and vegetables

recipe_benefits: High-quality beef provides complete protein and iron
Sweet potato fries offer healthier alternative to regular fries
Grilled vegetables add fiber and micronutrients
Balanced meal combining protein, carbs, and vegetables
Satisfying comfort food with nutritious upgrades
recipe_tips: Don't press down on burger while cooking - it releases juices
Let burger rest for 2-3 minutes after cooking for better texture
Sweet potato wedges get crispier if not overcrowded on the tray
Grill vegetables until they have nice char marks for extra flavor
Toast bun lightly for better texture and to prevent sogginess
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Classic comfort food elevated with sweet potato fries and grilled vegetables. The combination provides complete nutrition while satisfying burger cravings.

name: High-Protein Power Bowl with Lean plant-based meat & Sweet Potato
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and line a baking tray
Cube sweet potato and toss with half the olive oil, salt, and paprika
Roast sweet potato for 20-25 minutes until tender and lightly caramelized
Season beef cubes with garlic powder, black pepper, and remaining salt
Heat remaining olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat
Cook beef cubes for 6-8 minutes, turning to brown all sides
Let beef rest for 2 minutes to retain juices
Slice avocado and arrange in serving bowl
Add cottage cheese to one section of the bowl
Place roasted sweet potato and cooked beef in separate sections
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve immediately

benefits: Complete protein from beef and cottage cheese
Iron and zinc from lean beef

recipe_benefits: 35g complete protein supports muscle health and satiety
Lean beef provides iron, zinc, and vitamin B12
Cottage cheese delivers calcium and casein protein
Sweet potato offers fiber, potassium, and beta-carotene
Avocado provides healthy monounsaturated fats
Low carb with slow-releasing energy for blood sugar stability
Rich in vitamins and minerals for energy and health
recipe_tips: Choose lean cuts like sirloin or tenderloin for best protein-to-fat ratio
Don't overcook the beef - aim for medium for optimal tenderness
Sweet potato can be batch-cooked and reheated throughout the week
Full-fat cottage cheese provides better satiety and nutrient absorption
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Nutrient-dense power bowl emphasizing vitamins and minerals alongside optimal macros for sustained energy and health.

name: Marry me butter plant-based protein pasta
instructions: Cook whole wheat pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, cook chicken until golden and cooked through (6-8 minutes)
Remove chicken, add minced garlic to pan, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Return chicken to pan, mix well
Top with grated parmesan and serve immediately
recipe_benefits: 42g protein from chicken and dairy for muscle support
Whole wheat pasta provides sustained energy
Greek yogurt adds creaminess and probiotics
Sun-dried tomatoes boost flavor and antioxidants
Viral 'marry me' flavor that actually delivers nutrition
recipe_tips: Reserve pasta water to thin sauce if needed
Use full-fat Greek yogurt for creamiest texture
Butterfly chicken breast for faster cooking
Fresh spinach adds color and nutrition without overwhelming flavor
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Viral 'marry me' pasta combining butter chicken flavors with Italian comfort food. This protein-packed version keeps the indulgent taste while delivering serious nutrition.

name: Gluten-free lactose-free marry me butter plant-based protein pasta
instructions: Cook gluten-free pasta according to package directions (usually 1-2 minutes less than wheat pasta), drain and set aside
Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, cook chicken until golden and cooked through (6-8 minutes)
Remove chicken, add minced garlic to pan, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in lactose-free Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Return chicken to pan, mix well and serve immediately
recipe_benefits: All the viral flavor, none of the digestive issues
Sun-dried tomatoes add depth and antioxidants
recipe_tips: Gluten-free pasta can get mushy - cook al dente
Use certified gluten-free spice blends to avoid cross-contamination
Lactose-free yogurt behaves exactly like regular in this recipe
Reserve extra pasta water - gluten-free pasta absorbs more liquid
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Gluten-free and lactose-free version of the viral 'marry me' pasta. Perfect for those with celiac disease or lactose intolerance who don't want to miss out on trending recipes.

name: Creamy mushroom spinach gnocchi
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan and sauté the chopped onion until soft
Add mashed black garlic and fry for about 2 minutes
Add half of the butter and rosemary, cook briefly on low heat, then add remaining butter
Add mushrooms and cook until golden brown and moisture has released, season with salt and pepper
Add white wine vinegar and stir, then remove half the mushrooms and the rosemary, set aside for serving
Pour cream into the pan and stir in grated pecorino
Cook gnocchi according to package instructions, reserve 60ml cooking water
Add cooking water to the sauce and cook over medium heat until slightly thickened
Add spinach in batches and cook until wilted
Add drained gnocchi to the pan and cook for a few minutes to coat in sauce
Serve topped with reserved mushrooms and extra pecorino romano

benefits: Rich in B vitamins from mushrooms

recipe_tips: Black garlic adds a deep, umami-rich flavor but regular garlic works well too
Use a mix of mushroom varieties for complex flavor (chestnut, oyster, shiitake)
Reserve some mushrooms for topping to add texture contrast
Fresh gnocchi cooks faster than dried - check package instructions
recipe_notes: Rich and comforting Italian-style gnocchi with earthy mushrooms and fresh spinach. The pecorino adds a salty, tangy finish.

name: Creamy mushroom spinach gnocchi (lactose-free)
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan and sauté the chopped onion until soft
Add mashed black garlic and fry for about 2 minutes
Add half of the butter and rosemary, cook briefly on low heat, then add remaining butter
Add mushrooms and cook until golden brown and moisture has released, season with salt and pepper
Add white wine vinegar and stir, then remove half the mushrooms and the rosemary, set aside for serving
Pour cream into the pan and stir in grated pecorino
Cook gnocchi according to package instructions, reserve 60ml cooking water
Add cooking water to the sauce and cook over medium heat until slightly thickened
Add spinach in batches and cook until wilted
Add drained gnocchi to the pan and cook for a few minutes to coat in sauce
Serve topped with reserved mushrooms and extra pecorino romano

benefits: Rich in B vitamins from mushrooms

recipe_tips: Black garlic adds a deep, umami-rich flavor but regular garlic works well too
Use a mix of mushroom varieties for complex flavor (chestnut, oyster, shiitake)
Reserve some mushrooms for topping to add texture contrast
Fresh gnocchi cooks faster than dried - check package instructions
recipe_notes: Rich and comforting Italian-style gnocchi with earthy mushrooms and fresh spinach. The pecorino adds a salty, tangy finish.

name: Chocolate peanut butter energy bars
instructions: Line an 8-inch square pan with parchment paper and lightly oil using cooking spray
Preheat oven to 180°C
In a mixing bowl, combine quick oats, cocoa powder, peanut butter, water, and vanilla extract
Stir with a rubber spatula, then lightly oil your hands and squeeze the mixture to form a dough that is firm but not crumbly
If the mixture is too crumbly, add an extra 15ml water or olive oil and knead until it forms a ball
Press the dough into the prepared pan, using oiled fingers and a flat object like a jar lid to create an even layer
Bake for 12 minutes at 180°C on the center rack
Cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a cutting board using the parchment paper
Cut the warm bar into 12 pieces with a sharp knife
Melt dark chocolate chips with olive oil in a microwave in 30-second bursts, stirring between each
Drizzle melted chocolate over the cut bars and top with whole roasted almonds if desired
Place bars in the freezer for 12 minutes to set the chocolate

benefits: Contains antioxidants from dark chocolate and cocoa

recipe_tips: Use quick oats, not old-fashioned rolled oats, as they have the right texture
The dough should be firm but hold together - adjust water content as needed
Cut bars while still warm to prevent excessive crumbling
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week or freeze for longer storage
recipe_notes: Rich in plant-based protein from peanut butter and almonds. Dark chocolate provides flavanols for heart health. Makes a perfect pre- or post-workout snack.

name: Roasted sweet potato & chickpea bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Toss sweet potato cubes with 15ml olive oil, paprika powder, salt and pepper, spread on a baking tray lined with parchment paper
Toss chickpeas with 15ml olive oil, cumin, garlic powder, salt and pepper, spread on the same tray next to the sweet potatoes
Roast for 20-25 minutes until sweet potato is soft and lightly browned and chickpeas are slightly crispy, stir once halfway
Mix tahini, 15ml olive oil, lemon juice (about 23ml from 1 lemon), and water in a small bowl until smooth, add more water if too thick
Place spinach in a bowl and top with roasted sweet potato and chickpeas
Add sliced avocado, lemon slices, and sunflower seeds
Drizzle with remaining lemon juice and tahini dressing
Adjust seasoning with extra salt and pepper if needed

benefits: Rich in vitamins A and C

recipe_tips: Stir the vegetables halfway through roasting for even cooking
Make the dressing while the vegetables are roasting to save time
Add water gradually to the tahini dressing until you reach desired consistency
Can be meal prepped - store components separately and assemble when ready to eat
recipe_notes: Nutrient-dense bowl packed with plant-based protein, healthy fats from avocado and tahini, and complex carbs from sweet potato. Rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals.

name: Hojicha chia yogurt bowl
instructions: Mix plant-based milk with hojicha powder until dissolved, or steep 1 hojicha tea bag in warm milk and let cool
Stir in chia seeds and 5ml maple syrup, refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight until thickened
Make plum sauce by combining chopped plum, 2.5ml maple syrup, lemon juice, and water in a small pan
Simmer plum mixture for 5-7 minutes until soft and syrupy, set aside to cool
Prepare buckwheat crunch by mixing raw buckwheat groats, chopped nuts, sesame seeds, 2.5ml maple syrup, and a pinch of salt
Toast the buckwheat mixture in a dry pan over medium heat for 3-5 minutes until golden and fragrant, stirring frequently
Let buckwheat crunch cool completely to become crispy
Spoon plant-based yogurt into a bowl
Top with hojicha chia pudding, plum sauce, and buckwheat crunch
Garnish with fresh fig halves and red currants
recipe_tips: Make the chia pudding the night before for a quick morning assembly
Hojicha powder gives a unique roasted tea flavor - substitute with matcha if unavailable
Buckwheat crunch can be made in larger batches and stored in an airtight container
Any seasonal berries work well in place of red currants
Use regular yogurt if plant-based is not preferred
recipe_notes: A sophisticated Japanese-inspired breakfast bowl featuring roasted green tea, chia pudding, and homemade plum sauce. Rich in omega-3s from chia seeds and antioxidants from hojicha.
dutch_instructions: Mix plant-based milk with hojicha powder until dissolved, or steep 1 hojicha tea bag in warm milk and let cool
Stir in chia seeds and 5ml maple syrup, refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight until thickened
Make plum sauce by combining chopped plum, 2.5ml maple syrup, lemon juice, and water in a small pan
Simmer plum mixture for 5-7 minutes until soft and syrupy, set aside to cool
Prepare buckwheat crunch by mixing raw buckwheat groats, chopped nuts, sesame seeds, 2.5ml maple syrup, and a pinch of salt
Toast the buckwheat mixture in a dry pan over medium heat for 3-5 minutes until golden and fragrant, stirring frequently
Let buckwheat crunch cool completely to become crispy
Spoon plant-based yogurt into a bowl
Top with hojicha chia pudding, plum sauce, and buckwheat crunch
Garnish with fresh fig halves and red currants

name: Chocolate nut butter energy balls
instructions: Mash the banana in a mixing bowl until smooth
Add almond flour, nut butter, cinnamon, vanilla, salt, and maple syrup
Mix everything together until a sticky dough forms
Roll the mixture into 12 small balls and place on a parchment-lined tray
Chill in the refrigerator for 15 minutes to firm up
Melt the dark chocolate in a microwave in 30-second bursts, stirring between each
Roll each ball in the melted chocolate to coat
Return to the tray and chill for another 10 minutes until chocolate sets
recipe_tips: Use a ripe banana for natural sweetness and better binding
Any nut butter works - almond, peanut, or cashew
For a thinner chocolate coating, add 5ml coconut oil to the melted chocolate
Store in the refrigerator for up to 1 week
recipe_notes: These energy balls are rich in magnesium from almond flour and nut butter, making them supportive during menstrual and luteal phases. The banana provides potassium and natural sweetness.

name: Coffee tiramisu protein breakfast
instructions: In a jar or bowl, combine oats, plant-based milk, salt, cocoa powder, and chia seeds. Stir well to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix skyr with coffee-flavored protein powder until smooth. Add a splash of milk if the mixture is too thick.
Spoon the oat mixture into the bottom of a jar or bowl, then layer the protein cream on top.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight for best results.
Before serving, dust with extra cocoa powder and top with cacao nibs.
recipe_tips: If the mixture becomes too thick after chilling, stir in a splash of milk before eating
If it is too runny, add an extra teaspoon of chia seeds or reduce the milk by 20ml next time
Best prepared the night before for a quick grab-and-go breakfast
recipe_notes: A protein-rich twist on tiramisu flavors. The combination of coffee protein and cocoa creates a satisfying breakfast that feels like a treat. Oats and chia provide sustained energy.

name: Coffee caramel banana breakfast bowl
instructions: Pit the dates and blend with coconut yogurt, instant coffee, and a pinch of salt until smooth to create the coffee caramel cream.
Mash half the banana in a bowl and mix with oats, coconut milk, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt.
Spoon the oat mixture into a jar or bowl, then layer the coffee caramel cream on top.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight for best results.
Before serving, slice the remaining banana on top, add chopped hazelnuts and shaved dark chocolate.
Finish with a drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of flaky salt.
dutch_instructions: Ontpit de dadels en blend met kokosyoghurt, oploskoffie en een snufje zout tot een gladde koffie-karamelcrème
Prak de helft van de banaan in een kom en meng met havermout, kokosmelk, ahornsiroop, vanille-extract en een snufje zout
Schep het havermoutmengsel in een pot of kom en verdeel de koffie-karamelcrème erover
Dek af en zet minimaal 2 uur of een nacht in de koelkast voor het beste resultaat
Snijd voor het serveren de resterende banaan erbovenop, voeg gehakte hazelnoten en geschaafde pure chocolade toe
Werk af met een scheut olijfolie en een snufje vlokzout

name: Miso ginger orange jackfruit tacos
instructions: Drain and shred the jackfruit into pulled texture pieces.
In a bowl, whisk together orange juice, soy sauce, miso paste, grated ginger, maple syrup, minced garlic, black pepper, and chili flakes.
Heat 10ml olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add jackfruit and cook for 3-4 minutes until slightly caramelized.
Pour the miso-orange sauce over the jackfruit and simmer for 10-15 minutes until sauce thickens and jackfruit absorbs the flavors.
Make the salsa verde: blend remaining olive oil, capers, lemon juice and zest, and parsley until chunky.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan or microwave wrapped in a damp towel.
Fill tortillas with jackfruit, top with cheese and salsa verde.
Serve immediately with extra parsley.
recipe_tips: Use young green jackfruit in brine, not the sweet ripe version
Press jackfruit dry before cooking for better caramelization
The salsa verde can be made ahead and stored for 3 days
Use vegan cheese to keep it fully plant-based
recipe_notes: A plant-based twist on miso ginger tacos. Jackfruit shreds beautifully and absorbs the umami-rich miso-orange marinade. The salsa verde adds brightness and healthy fats.

name: Miso ginger orange mushroom tacos
instructions: Slice king oyster mushrooms lengthwise into thin strips, then shred with a fork to create a pulled texture.
In a bowl, whisk together orange juice, soy sauce, miso paste, grated ginger, maple syrup, minced garlic, black pepper, and chili flakes.
Heat 10ml olive oil in a pan over high heat. Add mushrooms and cook for 5-6 minutes until golden and slightly crispy.
Pour the miso-orange sauce over the mushrooms and cook for another 5 minutes until sauce caramelizes and coats the mushrooms.
Make the salsa verde: blend remaining olive oil, capers, lemon juice and zest, and parsley until chunky.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan or microwave wrapped in a damp towel.
Fill tortillas with mushrooms, top with cheese and salsa verde.
Serve immediately with extra parsley.
recipe_tips: King oyster mushrooms have the meatiest texture - shiitake works too but has a softer bite
Shredding the mushrooms with a fork creates a pulled-meat texture
Cook mushrooms on high heat without moving for the first 2 minutes to get crispy edges
Use vegan cheese and skip for fully plant-based version
recipe_notes: Meaty king oyster mushrooms soak up the umami miso-orange glaze beautifully. The high-heat cooking creates crispy edges while the inside stays tender. Rich in B vitamins and antioxidants.

name: Crispy smoked tofu and butterbean curry
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. In a small bowl, combine chickpea flour, turmeric, garam masala, paprika powder, cayenne, black pepper, and 1g salt.
Tear the pressed tofu into 2-3cm irregular pieces. Add to a bowl with the drained butterbeans and the spice mixture. Toss well to coat.
Spread evenly on a lined baking tray without overlapping. Bake for 18-20 minutes until crispy and golden at the edges.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a pan. Fry mustard seeds for 30 seconds until they pop. Add spring onion whites and a pinch of salt, fry 2 minutes.
Add garlic and fry 2-3 minutes, then add ginger and fry 2 minutes until the raw aroma subsides.
Add chopped chillies, coriander stalks, ground cumin and coriander. Fry 2 minutes with another pinch of salt.
Add diced tomatoes and remaining salt. Turn up heat until sizzling, then reduce to simmer. Cover and cook 8-10 minutes until tomatoes melt into the sauce.
Add 3/4 of the crispy tofu and beans to the sauce, reserving 1/4 for topping. Combine well.
Serve topped with remaining crispy tofu and beans, soy yogurt, fresh coriander, sesame seeds, and optional chilli flakes.
recipe_tips: Press the tofu well to remove excess liquid for maximum crispiness
Do not rinse the butterbeans - the starchy liquid helps the spices stick
The continuous salting at each stage builds layers of flavor
Serve with rice, naan, or roti for a complete meal
recipe_notes: A protein-packed curry with crispy baked tofu and butterbeans. The spice-coated beans and tofu get wonderfully crispy in the oven while the fresh tomato sauce stays bright and aromatic. Excellent source of plant protein and fiber.

name: Miso peanut butter banana bread
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk the ground flaxseed with warm water and set aside to thicken.
In a large mixing bowl, mash all the bananas until you have a thick pulpy texture.
Add the flax mixture, miso paste, peanut butter, maple syrup, vanilla, apple cider vinegar, and chia seeds. Mix until well combined, then add the soy milk and mix again.
Add the buckwheat flour, oats, baking powder, baking soda, and cinnamon. Mix together without overmixing, then fold in the hemp seeds and chopped dark chocolate.
The mixture should fall off your spoon in a second or two. If too stiff, add a splash more soy milk.
Preheat oven to 170°C. Pour the mix into a lined loaf tin and bake for 45-50 minutes, checking at 35 minutes. Cover with foil if the top is browning too quickly.
Allow to cool in the tin for at least an hour before slicing.
Serve toasted with extra peanut butter, berries, and a drizzle of maple syrup if desired.
recipe_tips: The bread is done when your house smells amazing - usually needs 3-5 more minutes after that
If the knife does not come out clean it might just be melted chocolate
Store at room temperature wrapped in foil for 2 days, then refrigerate for up to 4 more days
Toast slices for the best flavor and texture
recipe_notes: A nutrient-dense pre-workout snack packed with complex carbs from bananas and oats, plus protein from hemp seeds and peanut butter. The miso adds umami depth and gut-friendly probiotics. Perfect fuel before training.

name: Creamy sundried tomato orzo with tempeh (gluten-free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C.

Crumble the tempeh into small pieces onto a roasting tray. Drizzle with olive oil, tamari, garlic granules, and a pinch of salt. Toss well and flatten into an even layer. Bake for 16-18 minutes until golden and crispy at the edges.
In a large casserole pan, fry the shallot on low heat in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5-6 minutes. Add the garlic, paprika powder, turmeric, and dried thyme. Fry for 1-2 minutes.
Add the sundried tomatoes, gluten-free orzo, and spinach. Fry for a few minutes to toast the grains, then pour in all the vegetable stock.

Bring to a bubble, then cover and reduce to simmer for 4 minutes. Remove lid, stir well, making sure nothing sticks to the bottom. Continue cooking until the stock is absorbed (about 10 minutes). The mix should be wet and creamy.
Blend the silken tofu with lemon juice until smooth. Pour over the orzo mixture, sprinkle with nutritional yeast, add the chickpeas and fresh basil. Stir together and season to taste.
Ladle into bowls and top with the roasted tempeh, plenty of black pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil.
recipe_tips: Use a wide pan so the orzo cooks evenly - if using a narrower pot, add stock gradually
The silken tofu creates a creamy sauce without any dairy
Make sure to use certified gluten-free orzo pasta
recipe_notes: A creamy, protein-packed pasta dish with crispy roasted tempeh. The silken tofu blends into a luscious dairy-free sauce, while sundried tomatoes add Mediterranean depth. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based orzo.

name: Shiitake mushroom miso orzotto with smoked tofu (gluten-free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C.

Press the smoked tofu to remove excess water, then crumble into small pieces onto a roasting tray. Add 3 smaller shiitake mushrooms torn in half. Drizzle with olive oil, tamari, and garlic powder. Toss well and bake for 16-18 minutes until golden.

Finely chop or pulse the remaining mushrooms in a food processor until you have pieces around 0.5cm. Fry in a large casserole pan with olive oil and a pinch of salt on medium-high heat for 5-6 minutes until the steam subsides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, fry the shallot on low heat in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5-6 minutes. Add the garlic, dried thyme, and bay leaf. Fry for 1-2 minutes.
Add all the cavolo nero and fry until it turns a brighter green. Add the gluten-free orzo and toast for a few minutes, stirring regularly.

Pour in all the vegetable stock. Bring to a bubble, cover and simmer for 4 minutes. Remove lid, stir well, making sure nothing sticks. Continue cooking until stock is absorbed. The mix should be wet and creamy. Stir in the fried mushrooms.
Blend the silken tofu with miso and lemon juice until smooth. Pour over the orzo mixture, sprinkle with nutritional yeast, and stir together. Season to taste.
Ladle into bowls and top with the roasted tofu and shiitakes. Garnish with black pepper and a drizzle of olive oil.
recipe_tips: Use a wide pan so the orzo cooks evenly - if using a narrower pot, add stock gradually
The miso adds umami depth and gut-friendly probiotics
Make sure to use certified gluten-free orzo pasta
recipe_notes: A rich, umami-packed orzotto with crispy roasted smoked tofu and shiitake mushrooms. The silken tofu and miso create a creamy, probiotic-rich sauce. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based orzo.

name: Tenderstem broccoli orzotto with kale miso sauce (gluten-free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Rinse the tenderstem broccoli and add to a baking tray. Season with salt and pepper, drizzle with olive oil, and bake for 15 minutes. Allow to cool, then finely chop.
In a large wide casserole dish, fry the chopped shallot on low-medium heat in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5-6 minutes. Add the minced garlic, fry 2 minutes more.

Add the gluten-free orzo and toast the grains for 2-3 minutes. Pour in the vegetable stock, bring to a bubble, then reduce to a simmer for 10-12 minutes, or 2-3 minutes less than package suggests. You want it cooked but still soupy.

Meanwhile, make the kale miso sauce. Blanch the kale in boiling water, then quickly transfer to a bowl of iced water for several minutes. Drain, then add to a blender with the miso, lemon juice, and a splash of water. Blend until smooth, season to taste.
Turn off the heat, pour the edamame into the cooked orzo, then add the kale miso sauce and stir well. Gently fold in the tahini and chopped roasted broccoli.
Serve into bowls and top with lemon zest and a little more tahini.
recipe_tips: Use a wide pan so the orzo cooks evenly - if using a narrower pot, add stock gradually
The kale miso sauce adds probiotic benefits and umami depth
Pickled onions make a great optional topping
recipe_notes: A vibrant green orzotto with roasted tenderstem broccoli and a creamy kale miso sauce. Edamame adds complete plant protein while tahini brings calcium and creaminess. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based orzo.

name: Spanish-style tempeh orzo with chickpeas (gluten-free)
instructions: Crumble the tempeh into small chunks and fry in a non-stick pan on medium heat with a pinch of salt and olive oil for 6-8 minutes until browning at the edges. Remove and set aside.
In a large wide casserole dish, fry the chopped shallot and red pepper on low-medium heat in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5-6 minutes. Add the minced garlic and paprika, fry 2 minutes more.

Add the diced tomatoes and fry for a few more minutes. Add the gluten-free orzo and toast the grains for a minute. Pour in the stock, bring to a bubble, then reduce to simmer for 10-12 minutes, or 2-3 minutes less than package suggests. You want it cooked but still soupy.
Turn off the heat, pour the drained chickpeas, nutritional yeast, and 2/3 of the tempeh crumbles into the cooked orzo along with the balsamic vinegar. Gently fold in and season to taste.
Serve into bowls and top with soy yogurt mixed with water for a runny texture, remaining tempeh, fresh parsley, and a drizzle of olive oil.
recipe_tips: Use a wide pan so the orzo cooks evenly - if using a narrower pot, add stock gradually
The smoked paprika gives this dish its Spanish character
Double the recipe for easy meal prep
recipe_notes: A hearty Spanish-inspired orzotto with crispy tempeh crumbles and protein-rich chickpeas. The paprika powder and balsamic vinegar create deep, warming flavors. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based orzo.

name: Kale pesto pasta with tofu feta (gluten-free)
instructions: Make the tofu feta: crumble the tofu into small pieces (1-1.5cm) in a mixing bowl. Add half the lemon juice, 2 tbsp nutritional yeast, 1 crushed garlic clove, 1/2 tbsp oregano, 1 tsp olive oil, salt, black pepper, and chili flakes. Combine well and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Fry the shallot in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5 minutes, then add the remaining crushed garlic and fry for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
Boil a full kettle of water. Cook the gluten-free pasta in plenty of salted water for 1 minute less than packet suggests.
Quick blanch the kale by popping it into a sieve and running boiling water over it, then immediately running cold water over it. Squeeze out the water.

In a blender, add the blanched kale, drained cannellini beans, remaining lemon juice and nutritional yeast, fresh basil, the fried shallot and garlic, and about 100ml pasta water. Blend until very smooth (1-2 minutes). Season to taste.
Drain the pasta (reserving some pasta water). Combine with the sauce and stir well. Serve into bowls with the tofu feta on top, more black pepper and olive oil.
recipe_tips: Reserve pasta water to loosen the sauce if it thickens too much
The tofu feta is even better if made ahead and left to marinate
Use lentil or chickpea pasta for extra protein
recipe_notes: A vibrant green pasta with creamy kale pesto and tangy tofu feta. The cannellini beans blend into the sauce for extra creaminess and protein. This gluten-free version works beautifully with rice, corn, or legume-based pasta.

name: Tofu halloumi green bean curry
instructions: Press the tofu very well until quite dry. Cut into 1.5cm cubes and add to a mixing bowl with half the lemon juice, half the olive oil, dried italian herbs, half the sea salt, chili flakes, half the nutritional yeast, and 1/3 of the maple syrup. Stir well and refrigerate while preparing the curry.
Fry the onion in olive oil with a pinch of salt on medium heat. Add the garlic and ginger, fry 2-3 minutes until the raw aroma subsides.
Add all the spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, paprika powder, cinnamon), bay leaf, more salt, and a splash of olive oil. Fry for 1-2 minutes.
Add the chopped tomatoes, more salt, and the water. Stir well, bring to a bubble, then reduce to simmer on low heat while you fry the tofu.

Fry the marinated tofu on high heat with olive oil and a pinch of salt for 2-3 minutes each side until nicely charred. Remove from heat, pour over the remaining maple syrup, add more nutritional yeast and salt. Stir gently until absorbed.
Blanch the green beans in boiling water for 2-3 minutes, then plunge into ice water. Cut in half and add to the curry base.
Season the curry to taste, loosen with water if needed. Serve into bowls with rice, top with the tofu halloumi and toasted crushed almonds.
recipe_tips: Press the tofu very firmly for the best halloumi-like texture
The charred edges give the tofu an authentic halloumi taste
Toast the almonds in a dry pan for extra flavor
recipe_notes: A warming curry with crispy maple-glazed tofu that mimics the taste and texture of halloumi. The aromatic spice blend with garam masala and paprika powder creates depth, while fresh green beans add crunch. Naturally gluten-free.

name: Creamy roasted cauliflower gnocchi with smoked tofu (gluten-free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Break the cauliflower into small florets and spread over a baking tray. Toss with olive oil, salt, and black pepper. Roast for 20 minutes until browning at the edges.
Soak the cashews and sunflower seeds in boiling water for 10 minutes, then drain.

Chop the leeks and shallot. Heat olive oil in a large sauté pan. When the shallot sizzles on contact, add all the leek and shallot with some salt. Cook on medium heat for 10 minutes until soft and the steam subsides.

Make the creamy sauce: blend the soaked cashews, sunflower seeds, nutritional yeast, 1 clove garlic, lemon juice, salt, and water until very smooth. Add more water if too thick - it should be pourable like cream.
Cook the gluten-free gnocchi according to packet instructions, drain and set aside.
Add the remaining garlic and grated smoked tofu to the leek mixture and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add half the roasted cauliflower, the cooked gnocchi, and the creamy sauce. Stir to combine.
Serve into bowls topped with remaining roasted cauliflower, fresh parsley, olive oil drizzle, and black pepper.
recipe_tips: If allergic to nuts, substitute cashews with extra sunflower seeds
The sauce should be the consistency of double cream
Look for gnocchi made with rice flour or potato only
recipe_notes: A comforting creamy gnocchi dish with roasted cauliflower and grated smoked tofu. The cashew and sunflower seed sauce creates a rich, dairy-free cream. This gluten-free version uses gnocchi made without wheat flour.

name: Butterbean puttanesca with tofu feta (gluten-free)
instructions: Cook the gluten-free pasta for 1-2 minutes less than packet instructions suggest, in very salty water.
In a medium pan on medium heat, heat olive oil then add the minced garlic and fry until fragrant (about 1 minute - do not let it burn or it will be bitter).
Add the passata, capers, caper brine, and chopped kalamata olives. Gently heat until lightly bubbling and stir everything together for 5 minutes.

Make the tofu feta: in a mixing bowl, crumble the tofu and combine with nutritional yeast, lemon juice, dried oregano, 1g salt, 1 clove garlic minced, and 7ml olive oil. Stir well and season to taste.
When the pasta is done, add it straight into the sauce with a slotted spoon. Add the butterbeans and stir well. Add the torn basil, leaving a few leaves for garnish.
Serve into bowls with the tofu feta on top, more basil, a drizzle of olive oil, and plenty of black pepper.
recipe_tips: Do not burn the garlic or the dish will taste bitter
The caper brine adds essential saltiness and depth
Use legume pasta for extra protein if desired
recipe_notes: A bold Mediterranean pasta with briny olives, capers, and tangy tofu feta. The butterbeans add creaminess and protein to this classic Italian-inspired dish. This gluten-free version uses rice, corn, or legume-based pasta.

name: Miso tofu sushi bowl
instructions: Cook the sushi rice according to packet instructions if not already prepared.
Preheat oven to 200°C.

Grate the tofu onto a baking tray. Evenly distribute the paprika powder and cumin, drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with salt, and toss to combine. Spread out evenly and bake for 15 minutes until golden brown.
Mix the slaw: in a mixing bowl, add the diced avocado, diced cucumber, chopped spring onion, fresh coriander, sesame seeds, lime juice, and salt. Toss together and season to taste.

Sauté the aromatics: heat olive oil in a pan, add the shallot and sauté for 5 minutes with a pinch of salt. Add the garlic and ginger, cook for 2-3 minutes more until the raw aroma subsides. Turn the heat down.
Mix the miso paste with 2-3 tbsp water until smooth. Add to the pan with the aromatics, then transfer all the baked shredded tofu into the mix, coating well until no visible liquid remains.
Plate the bowls: start with sushi rice, then the miso tofu, the slaw, edamame beans, pickled ginger, lime wedge, and sriracha mayo. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
recipe_tips: Grating the tofu creates a fluffy texture that absorbs the spices beautifully
Use gluten-free miso paste and sriracha mayo to keep it gluten-free
The pickled ginger adds probiotic benefits
recipe_notes: A vibrant sushi-inspired bowl with crispy miso-glazed shredded tofu and fresh avocado-cucumber slaw. The combination of textures and the umami from miso paste makes this a satisfying, protein-rich meal. Naturally gluten-free.

name: Lentil lasagne with tofu bechamel (gluten-free)
instructions: In a wide oven-proof casserole dish, fry the onion and mushrooms in olive oil and salt until all their steam has subsided (about 10 mins). Add the garlic, sun-dried tomatoes and paprika powder. Fry 3 mins more, then add the tomato paste and cook for 3 mins until the paste has darkened.

Add the tinned tomatoes, fill the tin halfway with water to catch leftover tomato and add that too. Add the cooked lentils, soaked and drained walnuts (chopped roughly), salt and pepper. Break the tomatoes up with a spatula. Season to taste. Bring to the boil, then simmer on medium for 15-20 mins until reduced.
Meanwhile, make the tofu bechamel sauce: blend silken tofu, white miso paste, tahini, nutritional yeast, nutmeg, and lemon juice until smooth. Season to taste. Add a splash of water if too thick.

Add the balsamic vinegar to the pan, stir the sauce well. Pour 3/4 of the bechamel in an irregular pattern over the top. Stir lightly to combine but keep some contrast. Break up the gluten-free lasagne sheets with your hands and arrange randomly in the sauce. Cover any exposed pasta. Pour remaining bechamel over the top.
Put the lid on and cook on low for 10-12 mins. Meanwhile preheat the grill.
Check the pasta is almost cooked. Drizzle olive oil on top and grill for 3-5 mins until the top bubbles and chars slightly. Watch carefully to avoid burning.
Top with fresh basil, black pepper, and serve.
recipe_tips: Soaking the walnuts in boiling water for 10 minutes softens them
You can add 50g extra firm tofu to the bechamel for extra protein
The miso adds umami and probiotic benefits
recipe_notes: A hearty plant-based lasagne with rich lentil and mushroom ragu, topped with a creamy tofu-tahini bechamel sauce. The miso paste adds depth and gut-friendly fermented goodness. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based lasagne sheets.

name: Creamy butterbean stew with pea and mint sauce
instructions: Preheat the oven to 160°C.
Soak the cashews and pine nuts in boiling water.
Pop half the cherry tomatoes into a baking dish and coat with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast until the beans are ready.

Meanwhile, sauté the white parts of the spring onion in olive oil with a pinch of salt on medium heat for 3-4 mins. Add the garlic and fry 1-2 mins longer until the raw aroma subsides. Do not let it burn.
Add butterbeans with half their liquid from the can (or add water mixed with 1/2 tsp stock powder if draining). Simmer on low heat for 5 mins, season to taste.
Make the creamy pea and mint sauce: blend the peas, mint, basil, lemon juice, nutritional yeast, drained soaked nuts, olive oil, salt, and a splash of water until smooth. Season to taste.
Add the sauce to the pan with the beans, turn the heat off and combine well. Finish with the roasted tomatoes, green parts of the spring onion, and toasted seeds. Serve.
recipe_tips: You can swap the cashews and pine nuts for silken tofu if you prefer
The roasted tomatoes add sweetness and depth
Great served with sourdough toast
recipe_notes: A vibrant, fresh stew with creamy butterbeans and a bright pea, mint, and basil sauce. The roasted cherry tomatoes add pops of sweetness. Naturally gluten-free and packed with plant protein.

name: Miso spinach butterbeans with tofu feta
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C. Roast the cherry tomatoes in a baking tray with olive oil and salt for 10 minutes.

Prep the tofu feta: press the tofu in kitchen paper to drain excess water. Crumble into small irregular pieces (about 1cm). Add 7ml olive oil, 6g nutritional yeast, 1g dried oregano, 10ml lemon juice, 0.5 clove garlic minced, salt and pepper. Combine well and refrigerate while making the rest.
Blanch the spinach in boiling water, drain, then add to iced water and leave to sit.
Sauté the aromatics: in a frying pan on low heat, sauté the shallot for 5 minutes in olive oil with salt. Add the remaining garlic and fry 2 minutes more, then take off the heat to cool.
Make the sauce: drain the blanched spinach well. Blend the spinach, sautéed shallot and garlic, miso paste, remaining olive oil, remaining nutritional yeast, and remaining lemon juice until smooth.
In the same pan, heat the butterbeans with their liquid. Add the green sauce and stir through. Gently heat, then top with the roasted tomatoes, tofu feta, toasted seeds, and more olive oil.
recipe_tips: Make sure to drain the spinach well before blending for a thick sauce
The tofu feta benefits from marinating longer if you have time
Miso paste adds umami and probiotic benefits
recipe_notes: A vibrant green bowl with creamy butterbeans in a miso-spinach sauce, topped with tangy tofu feta and roasted cherry tomatoes. The fermented miso adds depth and gut-friendly benefits. Naturally gluten-free.

name: Tuscan butterbean stew with tofu ricotta
instructions: Make the tofu ricotta: in a blender, add the extra firm tofu, lemon juice, 1/3 of the garlic, 1 tsp salt, plant-based milk, and black pepper. Pulse 4-5 times for 2-3 seconds each until the texture resembles thick, rough ricotta. Season to taste and set aside.

Cook the veg: add olive oil to a large sauté pan and heat on medium until onion sizzles on contact. Add the leek, onion, and carrot with salt. Cook for 8 minutes, stirring regularly. Add the cavolo nero and cook for 3-4 more minutes.
Add the remaining garlic, all dried herbs and spices, bay leaf, salt and pepper. Stir well to coat the veg. Add the tomato paste and fry for 3 minutes.
Add the butterbeans with their liquid and the plum tomatoes, breaking them up with a spatula. Season with salt if needed and mix well.
Add the nutritional yeast and red wine vinegar, stir well, then take off the heat. Serve into bowls with big dollops of tofu ricotta and plenty of black pepper.
recipe_tips: Pulse the tofu briefly to keep a ricotta-like texture - do not over-blend
Great served with toasted sourdough bread
You can add tempeh to the pan with the kale for extra protein
recipe_notes: A hearty Italian-inspired stew with creamy butterbeans, tender vegetables, and aromatic herbs, topped with dollops of homemade tofu ricotta. The combination of paprika powder and bay leaf creates deep, comforting flavors. Naturally gluten-free.

name: Black bean quesadillas with cashew queso (gluten-free)
instructions: Make the bean mix: finely chop spring onions and 2/3 of the chilli. Fry in a pan with oil and salt for 5 mins. Add spice mix (dried coriander, cumin, cayenne) and 2/3 of the garlic, simmer for 1 minute. Add tomato paste and peanut butter, stir well. Add the beans with their liquid. Cook down for 10-15 mins.
Make the cashew queso: blend cashews, nutritional yeast, salt and pepper, 1/4 tsp paprika powder, 1/8 tsp turmeric, adding water to loosen as needed. Blend until very smooth (at least 30 seconds).
Prepare the guac: mash avocado with red onion, remaining garlic and chilli, olive oil, salt and pepper, lemon juice, and finely chopped fresh coriander. Set aside.

Make the quesadillas: add a gluten-free tortilla wrap to a hot pan, spread the bean mix evenly, top with queso and add another wrap on top. Wait until it smokes slightly, then flip with a plate and cook the other side.
Cut into slices and serve with queso and guac.
recipe_tips: Use corn-based or rice-based gluten-free tortillas for best results
The peanut butter adds richness to the bean filling
Blend the queso for at least 30 seconds for smooth texture
recipe_notes: Crispy Mexican-inspired quesadillas with spiced black beans, creamy cashew queso, and fresh guacamole. The combination of textures and bold flavors makes this a satisfying, protein-rich meal. This gluten-free version uses corn or rice-based tortillas.

name: Herb-infused vegetable lasagna with avocado oil drizzle (gluten-free)
instructions: Press and crumble tofu, season with nutritional yeast and herbs.
Sauté garlic and mushrooms in olive oil until fragrant.
Add cornstarch, then vegetable stock and cashew cream to create dairy-free sauce.
Blanch broccoli and spinach until bright green and tender-crisp.
Layer lasagna: sauce, tofu, vegetables, repeat with dairy-free layers.
Cover and bake at 180°C for 25-30 minutes until bubbly.
Drizzle with herb-infused avocado oil before serving.
recipe_tips: Cook gluten-free lasagna sheets according to packet instructions
Cornstarch creates a smooth sauce without gluten
recipe_notes: A vibrant vegetable lasagna with layers of seasoned tofu, mushrooms, broccoli and spinach in a creamy cashew sauce. This version uses gluten-free lasagna sheets and cornstarch for the sauce.

name: Herb-infused chicken lasagna with avocado oil drizzle (gluten-free)
instructions: Slice chicken breast in half and pan-fry both sides (5-6 mins) until golden.
Shred chicken and set aside to cool.
Sauté garlic and mushrooms in butter until fragrant.
Add cornstarch, then chicken stock, cottage cheese and cream cheese to create creamy sauce.
Blanch broccoli until bright green and tender-crisp.
Layer lasagna: sauce, chicken, cottage cheese mixture, broccoli, repeat.
Top with mozzarella and parmesan, grill for 3-5 minutes until bubbly.
Drizzle with herb-infused avocado oil before serving.
recipe_tips: Cook gluten-free lasagna sheets according to packet instructions
Cornstarch thickens the sauce without gluten
recipe_notes: A rich chicken lasagna with layers of shredded chicken, creamy cheese sauce, and broccoli. This version uses gluten-free lasagna sheets and cornstarch instead of flour.

name: Herb-infused chicken lasagna with avocado oil drizzle (lactose-free)
instructions: Slice chicken breast in half and pan-fry both sides (5-6 mins) until golden.
Shred chicken and set aside to cool.
Sauté garlic and mushrooms in lactose-free butter until fragrant.
Add flour, then chicken stock, lactose-free cottage cheese and cream cheese to create creamy sauce.
Blanch broccoli until bright green and tender-crisp.
Layer lasagna: sauce, chicken, cheese mixture, broccoli, repeat.
Top with lactose-free mozzarella and parmesan, grill for 3-5 minutes until bubbly.
Drizzle with herb-infused avocado oil before serving.
recipe_tips: Lactose-free cheese melts similarly to regular cheese
Look for aged parmesan which is naturally lower in lactose
recipe_notes: A rich chicken lasagna with layers of shredded chicken, creamy cheese sauce, and broccoli. This version uses all lactose-free dairy products for those with lactose intolerance.

name: Cassava tortillas (weekend meal prep)
instructions: Trim the ends off the cassava, then slice it into three pieces

Holding one piece in your hand, carefully score down the side - cutting through both layers of skin (the thick brown outer layer and the pinkish inner layer). Use your fingers to peel both layers off completely and discard them. Repeat with the remaining pieces
Chop the peeled cassava into smaller chunks, about 3-5cm in size, and place them in a saucepan of cold water

Bring to the boil, then cook for 25 minutes or until the cassava is completely soft all the way through - check by piercing with a knife; it should slide in easily and the flesh should look translucent, not chalky white. Make sure the cassava is completely cooked - this step is important because raw or undercooked cassava isn't safe to eat
Use a slotted spoon to remove the cassava and transfer to a plate. Leave for 5 minutes to cool slightly

Once cool enough to handle, remove and discard the fibrous core that runs through the centre of each piece. Transfer the cassava to a large bowl and mash until smooth, picking out any small pieces of core as you go
Add a pinch of salt if you like, then mix again with your hands to form a smooth, dough-like consistency
For small tortillas (ideal for tacos), divide into balls weighing around 70g each. For larger wraps, shape into 100g balls
Cut two square pieces of baking paper roughly 30cm wide. Place a cassava ball between them and roll out with a rolling pin until 2mm thick
Heat a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the tortilla and cook for 1-3 minutes on each side, until lightly charred and flexible
Transfer to a clean tea towel to cool while you cook the remaining tortillas
Roll out the next tortilla while one is cooking to keep things moving efficiently
recipe_tips: Yield: One average fresh cassava (around 1kg) makes about 6 small tortillas or 4 large ones
Where to buy: Fresh cassava is available in some large supermarkets, or you can find it in African, Caribbean, or Asian grocery stores
Peeling tip: Make sure to remove both layers of skin (brown and pink). Leaving any behind can make the cassava bitter and unsafe to eat
Cooking safety: Always boil cassava until fully soft before mashing. Undercooked cassava contains natural compounds that make raw cassava unsafe to eat
Storage: Keep cooked tortillas in an airtight container (separated with pieces of baking paper) in the fridge for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months
recipe_notes: These naturally gluten-free tortillas are made from just cassava and salt. Perfect for tacos, wraps, or as a side. Prepare on the weekend and use throughout the week.

Holding one piece in your hand, carefully score down the side - cutting through both layers of skin (the thick brown outer layer and the pinkish inner layer). Use your fingers to peel both layers off completely and discard them. Repeat with the remaining pieces
Chop the peeled cassava into smaller chunks, about 3-5cm in size, and place them in a saucepan of cold water

Bring to the boil, then cook for 25 minutes or until the cassava is completely soft all the way through - check by piercing with a knife; it should slide in easily and the flesh should look translucent, not chalky white. Once cool enough to handle, remove and discard the fibrous core that runs through the centre of each piece. Transfer the cassava to a large bowl and mash until smooth, picking out any small pieces of core as you go
Add a pinch of salt if you like, then mix again with your hands to form a smooth, dough-like consistency

Transfer to a clean tea towel to cool while you cook the remaining tortillas
Roll out the next tortilla while one is cooking to keep things moving efficiently

name: High protein peanut butter banana bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C and line a 9x5 inch loaf pan with parchment paper
In a large bowl, whisk together oat flour, protein powder, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon
In another bowl, mash the bananas until smooth
Add yogurt, melted coconut oil, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and eggs to the mashed bananas. Mix well
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined - don't overmix
Pour batter into the prepared loaf pan and smooth the top
Bake for 45-50 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean
Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely
Slice into 7 portions and store in an airtight container

benefits: Good source of potassium from bananas

recipe_tips: Use overripe bananas for maximum sweetness and moisture
The bread will continue to firm up as it cools
Store at room temperature for 2-3 days or refrigerate for up to 1 week
Can be frozen for up to 3 months - thaw at room temperature or toast slices
recipe_notes: A protein-packed twist on classic banana bread. With 17g protein per slice, it's perfect as a post-workout snack or satisfying breakfast on the go.
dutch_instructions: Verwarm de oven voor op 180°C en bekleed een 23x13 cm cakevorm met bakpapier.
Meng in een grote kom het havermeel, eiwitpoeder, baksoda, bakpoeder, zout en kaneel.
Prak in een andere kom de bananen tot glad.
Voeg yoghurt, gesmolten kokosolie, ahornsiroop, vanille-extract en eieren toe aan de geprakte bananen. Meng goed.
Giet de natte ingrediënten bij de droge ingrediënten en roer tot net gecombineerd - niet te lang mengen.
Giet het beslag in de voorbereide cakevorm en strijk het oppervlak glad.
Bak 45-50 minuten tot een prikker in het midden schoon uit komt.
Laat 10 minuten afkoelen in de vorm, breng dan over naar een rooster om volledig af te koelen.
Snijd in 7 porties en bewaar in een luchtdichte container.

name: High protein carrot cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C and line a 9x5 inch loaf pan with parchment paper
In a large bowl, whisk together oat flour, protein powder, baking soda, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, ginger, and nutmeg
Finely shred the carrots and set aside
In another bowl, mash the bananas until smooth
Add yogurt, melted coconut oil, maple syrup, vanilla paste, and eggs to the mashed bananas. Mix well
Stir in the shredded carrots
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined
Fold in the toasted pecans
Pour batter into the prepared loaf pan and smooth the top
Bake for 50-55 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean
Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely
Slice into 7 portions
recipe_tips: Toast the pecans before adding for extra flavor
Finely shred the carrots for better texture integration
Optional frosting: blend 120g dairy-free cream cheese, 15ml maple syrup, 7ml vanilla paste, and a pinch of sea salt
Store at room temperature for 2-3 days or refrigerate for up to 1 week
recipe_notes: A protein-rich carrot cake with warming spices. The carrots add moisture and beta-carotene for anti-aging benefits. Perfect as a nutritious snack or dessert.
dutch_instructions: Verwarm de oven voor op 180°C en bekleed een 23x13 cm cakevorm met bakpapier.
Meng in een grote kom het havermeel, eiwitpoeder, baksoda, bakpoeder, zout, kaneel, gember en nootmuskaat.
Rasp de wortels fijn en zet apart.
Prak in een andere kom de bananen tot glad.
Voeg yoghurt, gesmolten kokosolie, ahornsiroop, vanillepasta en eieren toe aan de geprakte bananen. Meng goed.
Roer de geraspte wortels erdoor.
Giet de natte ingrediënten bij de droge ingrediënten en roer tot net gecombineerd.
Spatel de geroosterde pecannoten erdoor.
Giet het beslag in de voorbereide cakevorm en strijk het oppervlak glad.
Bak 50-55 minuten tot een prikker in het midden schoon uit komt.
Laat 10 minuten afkoelen in de vorm, breng dan over naar een rooster om volledig af te koelen.
Snijd in 7 porties.

name: Matcha pistachio bliss balls
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine the almond flour, pistachio matcha crema, maple syrup, matcha powder, and vanilla extract
Stir until the mixture comes together into a soft, dough-like consistency
Roll the mixture into small bite-sized balls using your hands
Lightly dust each ball with matcha powder for garnish
Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 1 week
recipe_tips: Use high-quality matcha powder for best color and flavor
recipe_notes: These no-bake bliss balls are packed with healthy fats from almonds and pistachios.
dutch_instructions: Meng in een kom het amandelmeel, pistache-matcha crema, ahornsiroop, matchapoeder en vanille-extract.
Roer tot het mengsel een zachte, deegachtige consistentie krijgt.
Rol het mengsel met je handen tot kleine, hapklare balletjes.
Bestrooi elk balletje licht met matchapoeder als garnering.
Bewaar in een luchtdichte container in de koelkast tot 1 week.

name: Smashed potato tacos with guacamole
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line two baking trays
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add the potatoes and boil for 20 minutes or until soft. Drain and set aside for 5 minutes

Place the potatoes on the trays, ensuring none are touching, and use the base of a glass to press down on each potato, keeping them in one piece. Drizzle over olive oil and season well with salt and pepper
Bake in the oven for 45-50 minutes, or until golden and crispy. Do not turn over
While potatoes bake, prepare the guacamole by smashing the avocado with a fork
Dice the cherry tomatoes and drain the black beans
Once potatoes are crispy, top each with guacamole, black beans, diced tomatoes, and crumbled vegan feta
Garnish with fresh coriander and pickled onion
recipe_tips: Use small to medium potatoes for best results
Don't skip the boiling step - it ensures fluffy interiors
Make extra pickled onion to use throughout the week
The potatoes can be boiled ahead of time and smashed when ready to bake
recipe_notes: Crispy smashed potatoes serve as a gluten-free taco base. Loaded with fiber-rich black beans and heart-healthy avocado.
dutch_instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line two baking trays
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add the potatoes and boil for 20 minutes or until soft. Drain and set aside for 5 minutes

Place the potatoes on the trays, ensuring none are touching, and use the base of a glass to press down on each potato, keeping them in one piece. Drizzle over olive oil and season well with salt and pepper
Bake in the oven for 45-50 minutes, or until golden and crispy. Do not turn over
While potatoes bake, prepare the guacamole by smashing the avocado with a fork
Dice the cherry tomatoes and drain the black beans
Once potatoes are crispy, top each with guacamole, black beans, diced tomatoes, and crumbled vegan feta
Garnish with fresh coriander and pickled onion

name: Spiced date milk (winter warmer)
instructions: Remove the pits from the dates
Add the dates and plant-based milk to a blender
Add the ginger, cardamom, and cinnamon
Blend until completely smooth
Pour into a saucepan and heat gently until warm (do not boil)
Adjust the consistency with more milk if desired - it depends on the type of dates and how thick you want it
Pour into cups and serve warm
recipe_tips: Use soft Medjool dates for the creamiest result
Adjust spices to taste - start with less and add more
Can be served cold in summer as a smoothie
Store leftover date milk in the fridge for up to 3 days
recipe_notes: A traditional Middle Eastern warming drink perfect for cold winter evenings. Naturally sweetened with dates and spiced with warming ginger, cardamom, and cinnamon.
dutch_instructions: Verwijder de pitten uit de dadels.
Doe de dadels en plantaardige melk in een blender.
Voeg de gember, kardemom en kaneel toe.
Blend tot volledig glad.
Giet in een steelpan en verwarm zachtjes tot warm (niet koken).
Pas de consistentie aan met meer melk indien gewenst - dit hangt af van het type dadels en hoe dik je het wilt.
Schenk in kopjes en serveer warm.

name: Kidney bean and lean beef taco bowl
instructions: Brown the ground beef in a pan over medium-high heat for 5-7 minutes
Add cumin, paprika, garlic, chili flakes, salt and pepper, stir well
Add diced bell pepper and cook for 2-3 minutes
Add kidney beans and warm through for 1-2 minutes
Mash the avocado with a pinch of salt
Assemble bowl with beef mixture, top with cottage cheese, grated cheese, and mashed avocado
dutch_name: Kidneybonen en mager rundvlees taco bowl
dutch_instructions: Bak het rundergehakt in een pan op middelhoog vuur gedurende 5-7 minuten
Voeg komijn, paprika, knoflook, chilivlokken, zout en peper toe, roer goed
Voeg de paprika in blokjes toe en bak 2-3 minuten
Voeg kidneybonen toe en verwarm 1-2 minuten
Prak de avocado met een snufje zout
Maak de bowl met het vleesmengsel, top met hüttenkäse, geraspte kaas en gepureerde avocado

name: Kidney bean and lean beef tacos
instructions: Brown the ground beef in a pan over medium-high heat for 5-7 minutes
Add cumin, paprika, garlic, chili flakes, salt and pepper, stir well
Add diced bell pepper and cook for 2-3 minutes
Add kidney beans and warm through for 1-2 minutes
Mash the avocado with a pinch of salt
Warm the corn tortillas in a dry pan or microwave
Fill tortillas with beef mixture, top with cottage cheese, grated cheese, and mashed avocado
dutch_instructions: Bak het rundergehakt in een pan op middelhoog vuur gedurende 5-7 minuten
Voeg komijn, paprika, knoflook, chilivlokken, zout en peper toe, roer goed
Voeg de paprika in blokjes toe en bak 2-3 minuten
Voeg kidneybonen toe en verwarm 1-2 minuten
Prak de avocado met een snufje zout
Verwarm de maïstortilla's in een droge pan of magnetron
Vul de tortilla's met het vleesmengsel, top met hüttenkäse, geraspte kaas en gepureerde avocado

name: Shiitake lentil and kidney bean taco bowl
instructions: Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat
Add shiitake mushrooms and cook for 6-8 minutes until browned
Add soy sauce and cook 30-60 seconds to reduce slightly
Stir in cumin, paprika, garlic powder, and chili flakes
Add diced bell pepper and cook for 2-3 minutes
Add lentils and kidney beans, cook for 2-3 minutes until hot
Mash the avocado
Serve in a bowl topped with cottage cheese, grated cheese, and avocado
dutch_name: Shiitake linzen en kidneybonen taco bowl
dutch_instructions: Verhit olie in een pan op middelhoog vuur
Voeg shiitake paddenstoelen toe en bak 6-8 minuten tot ze bruin zijn
Voeg sojasaus toe en kook 30-60 seconden om iets in te koken
Roer komijn, paprika, knoflookpoeder en chilivlokken erdoor
Voeg de paprika in blokjes toe en bak 2-3 minuten
Voeg linzen en kidneybonen toe, verwarm 2-3 minuten
Prak de avocado
Serveer in een kom met hüttenkäse, geraspte kaas en avocado

name: Shiitake lentil and kidney bean tacos
instructions: Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat
Add shiitake mushrooms and cook for 6-8 minutes until browned
Add soy sauce and cook 30-60 seconds to reduce slightly
Stir in cumin, paprika, garlic powder, and chili flakes
Add diced bell pepper and cook for 2-3 minutes
Add lentils and kidney beans, cook for 2-3 minutes until hot
Warm the corn tortillas in a dry pan or microwave
Mash the avocado
Fill tortillas with the mixture, top with cottage cheese, grated cheese, and avocado

Voeg de paprika in blokjes toe en bak 2-3 minuten
Voeg linzen en kidneybonen toe, verwarm 2-3 minuten
Verwarm de maïstortilla's in een droge pan of magnetron
Prak de avocado
Vul de tortilla's met het mengsel, top met hüttenkäse, geraspte kaas en avocado

name: Baba ganoush
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Pierce aubergine several times with a fork and place on a baking tray
Roast in oven for 60 minutes until completely soft and collapsed
Let cool slightly, then scoop out the flesh and discard the skin
Mince the garlic clove
Add garlic, olive oil, lime juice, mustard and salt to the aubergine flesh
Mash and mix well until smooth but still has some texture
Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 5 days
recipe_benefits: Rich in fiber and antioxidants from aubergine
Anti-inflammatory properties
Prep once and use throughout the week
recipe_tips: Let aubergine cool before scooping for easier handling
Adjust lime and mustard to taste
Works great on sourdough toast, in wraps or as a dip
recipe_notes: Weekend prep component - use with sourdough toast, wraps or as a side
dutch_instructions: Verwarm de oven voor op 200°C
Prik de aubergine meerdere keren met een vork en leg op een bakplaat
Rooster in de oven gedurende 60 minuten tot helemaal zacht en ingezakt
Laat iets afkoelen, schep het vruchtvlees eruit en gooi de schil weg
Hak de knoflook fijn
Voeg knoflook, olijfolie, limoensap, mosterd en zout toe aan het auberginevlees
Stamp en meng goed tot glad maar nog met wat textuur
Bewaar in een luchtdichte container in de koelkast tot 5 dagen

name: Chocolate protein pudding
instructions: Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth
Pour into a glass or jar
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour or overnight until set
Serve chilled, optionally topped with banana slices and cacao nibs

benefits: Rich in omega-3 from chia and hemp seeds

recipe_benefits: High in plant-based protein from hemp seeds
Rich in omega-3 fatty acids from chia seeds
Cacao provides antioxidants and cocoa flavanols
Prep overnight and grab-and-go in the morning
recipe_tips: Blend well for a smooth texture
Chia seeds will thicken the pudding as it sets
Lasts 3 days in the fridge once prepared
recipe_notes: Weekend prep breakfast - blend and refrigerate overnight for a quick morning meal
dutch_instructions: Doe alle ingrediënten in een blender en mix tot een glad geheel
Giet in een glas of pot
Zet minimaal 1 uur of een nacht in de koelkast tot het is opgestijfd
Serveer gekoeld, eventueel met plakjes banaan en cacaonibs

name: High-protein overnight oats with berries
instructions: Mix oats, chia seeds, and protein powder in a jar or container
Add plant-based milk and almond butter, stir well
Add vanilla extract and stevia to taste
Top with mixed berries
Cover and refrigerate overnight (minimum 4 hours)
Stir before eating, add more berries if desired

name: Purple Sweet Potato Air Fryer Balls
instructions: Peel and slice sweet potato into small cubes. Bring a steamer to a boil and steam the sweet potato cubes for 10-12 minutes or until softened.
Mash the steamed sweet potatoes and combine with tapioca starch, coconut milk, and baking powder.
Roll the dough into small balls, approximately 24 grams each.
Lightly grease an air fryer and place the dough balls in. Air fry at 175°C (350°F) for 15-17 minutes.
Enjoy as is or serve with some agave/honey and sesame seeds.
recipe_benefits: Rich in antioxidants from purple sweet potatoes
Lower fat alternative to traditional fried snacks
recipe_tips: You can freeze the uncooked dough balls and air fry at a later time.
recipe_notes: Purple sweet potatoes (Okinawan) are rich in anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants. Regular sweet potatoes can be substituted.
